Consider using a more specific path or pattern.)","metadata":{"count":100,"truncated":true},"title":"","time":{"start":1780205284558,"end":1780205284637}},"id":"prt_e7c80ecc5001h06kd3Z43ILnLb","sessionID":"ses_1838070fafferbmuIbMyAqlS9c","messageID":"msg_e7c80bc17001cYehspZgESjQPT"}}
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-001.out:2818:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/122-deep-improvement-skill-benchmark-mode/006-deep-review/review/hs/logs/iter-009.out:43:{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1780205290663,"sessionID":"ses_1838070fafferbmuIbMyAqlS9c","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"call_function_4vnhn0n98zpe_1","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md"},"output":"<path>/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: ---\n2: name: deep-loop-runtime\n3: version: 1.4.0\n4: description: \"Shared deep-loop runtime: executor + prompt-pack + validation + atomic state + coverage-graph + Bayesian scoring + fallback routing.\"\n5: allowed-tools: [Bash, Read, Glob, Grep]\n6: ---\n7: \n8: # Deep Loop Runtime\n9: \n10: Shared runtime infrastructure for the deep-review and deep-research loop workflows. The FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP consolidation moved the shared runtime libraries, coverage-graph ownership, script entry points, SQLite storage, and runtime tests into this peer skill.\n11: \n12: ---\n13: \n14: ## 1. WHEN TO USE\n15: \n16: This skill is consumed by deep-review and deep-research workflow YAML and script paths, not invoked directly as a user-facing workflow.\n17: \n18: Use it when a deep-loop workflow needs shared runtime support for:\n19: \n20: - executor configuration and audit metadata\n21: - prompt-pack rendering\n22: - post-dispatch validation\n23: - atomic state files and JSONL repair\n24: - loop locking and permissions gates\n25: - Bayesian scoring and fallback routing\n26: - coverage-graph schema, query, and signal interpretation\n27: \n28: ### Activation Triggers\n29: \n30: This skill is activated when:\n31: \n32: - A deep-review or deep-research workflow YAML invokes `node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/<name>.cjs`\n33: - A test file imports `from '.../deep-loop-runtime/lib/...'`\n34: - An operator queries / mutates the coverage graph via the runtime scripts\n35: - A workflow needs Bayesian convergence scoring or fallback executor routing\n36: \n37: ### When NOT to Use\n38: \n39: Do not invoke this skill when:\n40: \n41: - A user requests an interactive command (use `/deep:start-review-loop` or `/deep:start-research-loop` instead — those orchestrate this runtime)\n42: - A package outside deep-review / deep-research needs deep-loop semantics (out of supported scope; open a packet to extend ownership)\n43: - An MCP tool surface is needed (this skill explicitly has none — the isolation ADR removed the deep-loop MCP tools)\n44: \n45: ---\n46: \n47: ## 2. SMART ROUTING\n48: \n49: This skill has no direct LLM-facing keyword triggers — it is invoked by workflow YAMLs (via `bash:` script calls) and by test imports. The router below documents which sub-domain to load for a given consumer query.\n50: \n51: ### Primary Detection Signal\n52: \n53: ```bash\n54: request_text=\"$(printf '%s' \"$USER_REQUEST\" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')\"\n55: case \"$request_text\" in\n56:   *\"deep-loop runtime\"*|*\"deep-loop-runtime\"*|*\"executor config\"*|*\"prompt pack\"*) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=1 ;;\n57:   *\"coverage graph\"*|*\"deep_loop_graph\"*|*\"bayesian scorer\"*|*\"loop lock\"*) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=1 ;;\n58:   *\"fallback router\"*|*\"post-dispatch validate\"*|*\"jsonl repair\"*) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=1 ;;\n59:   *) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=0 ;;\n60: esac\n61: ```\n62: \n63: ### Phase Detection\n64: \n65: ```text\n66: TASK CONTEXT\n67:     |\n68:     +- STEP 0: Detect runtime-call intent vs. consumer-orchestration intent\n69:     +- STEP 1: If consumer (workflow YAML), invoke the relevant scripts/*.cjs\n70:     +- STEP 2: If contributor (modifying lib code), load matching lib/ subtree\n71:     +- STEP 3: If test author, load tests/ + lib/ pair\n72: ```\n73: \n74: ### Resource Domains\n75: \n76: ```text\n77: lib/deep-loop/*.ts            # 10 runtime modules (executor, prompt-pack, validation, state, scoring, routing)\n78: lib/coverage-graph/*.ts       # 3 schema + query + signals modules\n79: scripts/*.cjs                 # 4 entry points (convergence, upsert, query, status)\n80: database/*.sqlite              # runtime-owned SQLite (deep-loop-graph.sqlite)\n81: tests/{unit,integration,lifecycle}/  # 21+ vitest files split by responsibility\n82: ```\n83: \n84: ### Resource Loading Levels\n85: \n86: | Tier | When loaded | Purpose |\n87: |------|-------------|---------|\n88: | ALWAYS | SKILL.md frontmatter | description, allowed-tools, version |\n89: | CONDITIONAL | When a runtime call dispatches | `lib/` modules required by the called function |\n90: | ON_DEMAND | When a test fails or a contributor extends | `tests/` + matching `lib/` sub-tree |\n91: \n92: ### Smart Router Pseudocode\n93: \n94: ```text\n95: IF request mentions a specific lib name (e.g. \"bayesian-scorer\", \"prompt-pack\"):\n96:   → Load lib/deep-loop/<name>.ts + matching tests/unit/<name>.vitest.ts\n97: ELIF request mentions coverage-graph / convergence / signals:\n98:   → Load lib/coverage-graph/*.ts + tests/integration/review-depth-*.vitest.ts\n99: ELIF request mentions a script (convergence.cjs, status.cjs, etc.):\n100:   → Load scripts/<name>.cjs + tests/integration/<name>-script.vitest.ts + lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts\n101: ELSE:\n102:   → Defer to consumer skill (deep-review / deep-research)\n103: ```\n104: \n105: ---\n106: \n107: ## 3. HOW IT WORKS\n108: \n109: ### Runtime Architecture\n110: \n111: The runtime exposes deep-loop primitives to consumer workflows through two paths: **direct `.cjs` script invocation** (replacing the removed `mcp__mk_spec_memory__deep_loop_graph_*` MCP tools) and **TypeScript library imports** (from workflow scripts and tests).\n112: \n113: ```text\n114: [deep-review/deep-research workflow YAML]\n115:             │\n116:             ├─ bash: node .../scripts/<name>.cjs   (coverage-graph mutations)\n117:             │       └─→ lib/coverage-graph/*.ts → database/deep-loop-graph.sqlite\n118:             │\n119:             └─ ts-import: lib/deep-loop/<lib>.ts   (runtime primitives)\n120:                     └─→ atomic-state, prompt-pack, post-dispatch-validate, etc.\n121: ```\n122: \n123: ### Library Structure\n124: \n125: **`lib/deep-loop/` (10 modules):**\n126: - `executor-config.ts` — schema + parsing for per-iteration executor settings\n127: - `executor-audit.ts` — appends `executor` block to iteration JSONL (provenance)\n128: - `prompt-pack.ts` — renders the iteration prompt template\n129: - `post-dispatch-validate.ts` — validates iteration outputs (markdown + JSONL + delta)\n130: - `atomic-state.ts` — atomic state-log writes\n131: - `jsonl-repair.ts` — recovers corrupt JSONL state lines\n132: - `loop-lock.ts` — single-writer locking\n133: - `permissions-gate.ts` — permission scope checks\n134: - `bayesian-scorer.ts` — convergence scoring\n135: - `fallback-router.ts` — executor fallback decision matrix\n136: \n137: **`lib/coverage-graph/` (3 modules):**\n138: - `coverage-graph-db.ts` — SQLite schema + node-kind allow-list + connection lifecycle\n139: - `coverage-graph-query.ts` — query builders\n140: - `coverage-graph-signals.ts` — convergence signal extraction\n141: \n142: ### Council Primitives\n143: \n144: The Runtime Boundary Decision (ADR-001) extends `deep-loop-runtime/lib/` with council-compatible infrastructure primitives while keeping operator-facing and domain workflow semantics in `deep-ai-council`. These primitives are consumed by downstream deep-ai-council orchestration (per-topic multi-round, session-findings registry, command and skill wiring, parity and cost docs) for per-topic orchestration, multi-topic session state, command wiring, parity tests and docs.\n145: \n146: **`lib/council/` (5 modules):**\n147: - `multi-seat-dispatch.cjs` — runs seat executors in parallel for one council round, preserves seat result order, and returns fulfilled/rejected per-seat outcomes plus round summary counts.\n148: - `round-state-jsonl.cjs` — appends per-round JSONL records with a lock-file single-writer guard, repairs corrupt trailing JSONL before append, fsyncs writes, and exposes round-state readers for resume.\n149: - `adjudicator-verdict-scoring.cjs` — scores Round-N -> Round-N+1 adjudicator verdict deltas using ADR-003 weights for option change, confidence delta, material-risk Jaccard delta, axis flip rate, and blocking-disagreement delta.\n150: - `cost-guards.cjs` — normalizes and enforces ADR-004 defaults for `max_rounds_per_topic`, `max_topics_per_session`, `saturation_threshold`, and `seats_per_round`, including upper-bound seat-output calculation.\n151: - `session-state-hierarchy.cjs` — creates and validates the ADR-002 session -> topic -> round state shape, including stable `topic-NNN-slug` and `round-NNN` ids.\n152: \n153: The existing `lib/deep-loop/atomic-state.ts`, `lib/deep-loop/jsonl-repair.ts`, and `lib/deep-loop/loop-lock.ts` remain the sibling runtime contracts. Council modules mirror those durability semantics in a council-scoped CJS surface so downstream `deep-ai-council` phases can consume them without modifying deep-review or deep-research behavior.\n154: \n155: ### Script Entry Points\n156: \n157: Four `.cjs` scripts replace the 4 deleted MCP tools, each honoring the same JSON-in / JSON-out contract documented in ADR-001 (the script interface contract):\n158: \n159: | Script | Replaces | Purpose | Exit codes |\n160: |--------|----------|---------|------------|\n161: | `scripts/convergence.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_convergence` | Computes typed CONTINUE / STOP_ALLOWED / STOP_BLOCKED decisions | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |\n162: | `scripts/upsert.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_upsert` | Stores nodes + edges from iteration `graphEvents` | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |\n163: | `scripts/query.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_query` | Inspects uncovered questions, unverified claims, contradictions | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |\n164: | `scripts/status.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_status` | Session-scoped health report | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |\n165: | `scripts/fanout-pool.cjs` | n/a (new) | Concurrency-capped worker pool + status ledger for fan-out lineages | exports only (no main) |\n166: | `scripts/fanout-run.cjs` | n/a (new) | CLI lineage pool driver — spawns N headless CLI subprocesses (codex, claude, opencode, gemini, devin), each running the full loop in `lineages/{label}/`; salvage sweep via fanout-salvage.cjs after each subprocess exits | 0=all ok, 2=some failed, 3=all failed |\n167: | `scripts/fanout-salvage.cjs` | n/a (new) | Write-failure salvage: recovers missing iteration .md files from captured subprocess stdout; per-sessionId isolation preserved | exports only (no main) |\n168: | `scripts/fanout-merge.cjs` | n/a (new) | Cross-lineage merge: research (dedup by id + attribution) or review (strongest-restriction P0 rollup) → consolidated registry + fanout-attribution.md | 0=ok, 3=input |\n169: \n170: **Fan-out isolation invariant:** each CLI lineage receives its own `{base}/lineages/{label}/` artifact dir (via `config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir` override in `step_resolve_artifact_root`) and its own `session_id` for `convergence.cjs` + coverage-graph DB writes (PK includes session_id — no schema change, no collision). Same-kind replicas get distinct `SPECKIT_<KIND>_STATE_DIR` values to prevent lockfile contention.\n171: \n172: Each script: parses argv → opens `database/deep-loop-graph.sqlite` → calls the lib function → writes JSON to stdout → closes DB in `finally` → exits with the appropriate code.\n173: \n174: ### Test Organization\n175: \n176: ```text\n177: tests/\n178: ├── unit/         # 13 files — per-module unit tests (atomic-state, executor-config, etc.)\n179: ├── integration/  # 7 files — script invocations + review-depth fixtures\n180: ├── lifecycle/    # 1 file — db-open-close pattern verification\n181: └── _helpers/     # 1 file — spawn-cjs helper for direct-invocation tests\n182: ```\n183: \n184: The system-spec-kit `mcp_server/vitest.config.ts` includes `'../deep-loop-runtime/tests/**/*.{vitest,test}.ts'` so a single vitest run discovers both surfaces.\n185: \n186: ---\n187: \n188: ## 4. RULES\n189: \n190: ### ALWAYS\n191: \n192: - **ALWAYS** open the SQLite DB inside a `try` block and close it in a `finally` block (script lifecycle invariant per ADR-001, the script interface contract)\n193: - **ALWAYS** emit JSON-only output to stdout from `scripts/*.cjs` (workflow YAML parses stdout for output bindings)\n194: - **ALWAYS** include `'use strict';` on line 2 of every `.cjs` script (per sk-code OPENCODE JS style)\n195: - **ALWAYS** include a `MODULE: <name>` header comment in every `lib/**/*.ts` file (per sk-code OPENCODE TS-MODULE-HEADER convention)\n196: - **ALWAYS** preserve atomic-state semantics when mutating JSONL state — never partial-write\n197: - **ALWAYS** acquire `loop-lock` before any state-log mutation\n198: \n199: ### NEVER\n200: \n201: - **NEVER** register MCP tools from this skill — the isolation ADR explicitly removed the MCP surface; reintroducing tools defeats the FULL_ISOLATE direction\n202: - **NEVER** open the SQLite DB outside `lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts` — single owner of the connection\n203: - **NEVER** import from `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/deep-loop/` or `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/` — those locations are empty after the runtime moved here; import from this skill instead\n204: - **NEVER** write to `database/deep-loop-graph.sqlite` without holding the `loop-lock`\n205: - **NEVER** swallow exceptions in script entry points — propagate to the exit-code matrix\n206: \n207: ### ESCALATE IF\n208: \n209: - **ESCALATE** to the user if a runtime call requires a new MCP tool — the architectural direction prohibits MCP additions; the user must decide on policy override\n210: - **ESCALATE** to a new ownership ADR if a new consumer skill (beyond deep-review and deep-research) needs deep-loop runtime — extension requires a new ownership ADR\n211: - **ESCALATE** if the SQLite schema requires a backward-incompatible change — coordinate with all consumer workflows + add migration to `coverage-graph-db.ts`\n212: - **ESCALATE** if a script's stdout JSON contract changes — every workflow YAML output binding depends on the existing shape\n213: \n214: ---\n215: \n216: ## 5. SCOPE\n217: \n218: In scope:\n219: \n220: - runtime libraries under `lib/deep-loop/`\n221: - coverage-graph runtime libraries under `lib/coverage-graph/`\n222: - `.cjs` script entry points under `scripts/`\n223: - runtime-owned SQLite storage under `database/`\n224: - runtime tests under `tests/`\n225: \n226: Out of scope:\n227: \n228: - MCP tool registration\n229: - MCP handler ownership\n230: - DB lifecycle inside `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/`\n231: - direct user command routing\n232: \n233: ---\n234: \n235: ## 6. ARCHITECTURE\n236: \n237: - `lib/deep-loop/` holds shared deep-loop runtime libraries.\n238: - `lib/coverage-graph/` holds coverage-graph schema, query, and signal helpers.\n239: - `scripts/` holds `.cjs` entry points used by workflow YAML and collateral commands.\n240: - `database/` owns relocated runtime SQLite artifacts.\n241: - `tests/` holds runtime-owned tests split from the MCP server surface.\n242: \n243: Shipped layout:\n244: \n245: | Path | Populated By | Purpose |\n246: |------|--------------|---------|\n247: | `lib/deep-loop/` | 10 files | Shared executor config, audit, prompt-pack, validation, state, repair, locking, permissions, scoring, and fallback routing. |\n248: | `lib/coverage-graph/` | 3 files | Coverage-graph schema owner, query helpers, and signal interpretation. |\n249: | `scripts/` | 4 files | Direct `status`, `query`, `upsert`, and `convergence` entry points replacing the removed deep-loop MCP tools. |\n250: | `database/` | SQLite | Runtime-owned `deep-loop-graph.sqlite`. |\n251: | `tests/` | Unit, integration, lifecycle | Runtime-owned tests discovered by the system-spec-kit MCP server Vitest config. |\n252: \n253: ---\n254: \n255: ## 7. INTEGRATION POINTS\n256: \n257: - **ADR-001 (AI Council SPLIT ruling)**: original AI Council SPLIT ruling. Superseded by user directive.\n258: - **Isolation ADR (FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP)**: Moves deep-loop runtime, coverage-graph schema/query/signals, script entry points, storage, and tests into this peer skill while removing the deep-loop MCP tool surface.\n259: - **Deep-review and deep-research workflow YAML** call this runtime through script entry points after the runtime cutover.\n260: - **system-spec-kit/mcp_server/vitest.config.ts** includes `'../deep-loop-runtime/tests/**/*.{vitest,test}.ts'` for cross-package test discovery.\n261: - **deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs** depends on the moved coverage-graph runtime via lib imports.\n262: \n263: ---\n264: \n265: ## 8. SUCCESS CRITERIA\n266: \n267: The runtime is healthy when:\n268: \n269: - Every `scripts/` entry point (`status`, `query`, `upsert`, `convergence`) returns valid JSON on its documented stdout contract\n270: - Consumer workflows resolve their `lib/` imports without path errors\n271: - The runtime-owned SQLite under `database/` is present and its coverage-graph schema matches `coverage-graph-db.ts`\n272: - Runtime tests under `tests/` pass through the system-spec-kit Vitest discovery glob\n273: \n274: ---\n275: \n276: ## 9. REFERENCES AND RELATED RESOURCES\n277: \n278: - ADR-001 (script interface contract): documents the script-shim and DB-relocation contract.\n279: - Isolation ADR (FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP): documents the MCP tool surface removal rationale.\n280: - Decision rationale lives in this skill's changelog and originating decision records.\n281: \n282: Related resources:\n283: \n284: - Consumer skills: the `deep-review` and `deep-research` workflows call this runtime through the script entry points\n285: - `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/vitest.config.ts` discovers this skill's `tests/`\n286: - Per-release detail and rationale: this skill's `changelog/` directory\n\n(End of file - total 286 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"---\nname: deep-loop-runtime\nversion: 1.4.0\ndescription: \"Shared deep-loop runtime: executor + prompt-pack + validation + atomic state + coverage-graph + Bayesian scoring + fallback routing.\"\nallowed-tools: [Bash, Read, Glob, Grep]\n---\n\n# Deep Loop Runtime\n\nShared runtime infrastructure for the deep-review and deep-research loop workflows. The FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP consolidation moved the shared runtime libraries, coverage-graph ownership, script entry points, SQLite storage, and runtime tests into this peer skill.\n\n---\n\n## 1. WHEN TO USE\n\nThis skill is consumed by deep-review and deep-research workflow YAML and script paths, not invoked directly as a user-facing workflow.\n\nUse it when a deep-loop workflow needs shared runtime support for:\n\n- executor configuration and audit metadata","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md","time":{"start":1780205290639,"end":1780205290662}},"id":"prt_e7c81029a0018WgctXtXLcmFh5","sessionID":"ses_1838070fafferbmuIbMyAqlS9c","messageID":"msg_e7c80eee8001ZiIO6I4pjCr85X"}}

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-001.out:3880:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/004-code-graph/011-source-bug-and-misalignment-audit/review-r2-opus/deep-review-findings-registry.json:33:      "title": "Sole concurrency test exercises the wx fresh-lease path, not the DR-002-01 reclaim re-read CAS",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-001.out:3881:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/004-code-graph/011-source-bug-and-misalignment-audit/review-r2-opus/deep-review-findings-registry.json:34:      "why": "The DR-002-01 fix added a re-read-after-write CAS to the RECLAIM branch of acquireOwnerLeaseFile (mk-code-index-launcher.cjs lines 373-386), which runs only when an `existing` stale owner lease is present. The only concurrency regression test, 'lets exactly one concurrent launcher own the lease' (line 207), spawns two launchers from a fresh createWorkspace() (tmpdir, no seeded owner lease, line 24-42). With no `existing` lease, both launchers take the `writeOwnerLeaseFileExclusive` (O_EXCL `wx`) fresh path (lines 360-371), whose single-winner guarantee comes entirely from O_EXCL and predates DR-002-01. The reclaim-path re-read CAS (the actual code DR-002-01 changed) is never reached: 'reclaims a stale-heartbeat owner lease with a live PID' (line 309) and 'reclaims a dead-pid lease' (line 296) each spawn only ONE launcher against a pre-seeded stale lease, so no two processes ever concurrently hit the reclaim branch. The test therefore passes identically against the un-fixed code (delete lines 377-384 and 'exactly one concurrent launcher' still goes green), giving false confidence that the reclaim CAS is verified.",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-001.out:3882:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/004-code-graph/011-source-bug-and-misalignment-audit/review-r2-opus/deep-review-findings-registry.json:142:      "title": "Owner-lease state machine is duplicated across launcher.cjs and owner-lease.ts with DIVERGENT concurrency primitives",

snapshot closure and P0/P1 contradiction check","questionsCovered":["Q-P0","Q-P1","Q-P2","Q-OPP","Q-ARCH"],"sourcesRead":[".opencode/skills/sk-deep-research/SKILL.md",".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/SKILL.md",".opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/014-mcp-runtime-stress-remediation/011-post-stress-finding-remediation-research/research/deep-research-strategy.md",".opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/014-mcp-runtime-stress-remediation/011-post-stress-finding-remediation-research/research/iterations/iteration-005.md",".opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/014-mcp-runtime-stress-remediation/011-post-stress-finding-remediation-research/research/iterations/iteration-006.md",".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/code_graph/handlers/status.ts",".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/code_graph/handlers/query.ts",".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/code_graph/handlers/README.md",".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/code_graph/lib/ensure-ready.ts",".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/code_graph/lib/readiness-contract.ts",".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/ops/file-watcher.ts",".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts",".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/code-graph-query-fallback-decision.vitest.ts",".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/ensure-ready.vitest.ts",".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/deep-loop/executor-config.vitest.ts",".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/code_graph/tests/code-graph-siblings-readiness.vitest.ts"],"keyFindings":["Q-P0 winner remains command-owned Copilot target authority because resolveCopilotPromptArg still only chooses raw prompt versus @PROMPT_PATH and has no approved-folder or plan-only Gate 3 contract.","Q-P1 winner remains an isolated SPEC_KIT_DB_DIR degraded-graph harness because unit coverage already exercises all fallbackDecision branches while the stress gap was end-to-end degraded state.","Q-P2 winner is a new non-mutating getGraphReadinessSnapshot helper for code_graph_status; getGraphFreshness currently loses action/reason detail, while ensureCodeGraphReady is unsuitable because it caches, cleans deleted tracked files, and can index.","Do not lower file-watcher debounce first; existing debounce/write-stability/concurrency/dedupe behavior makes status action visibility the lower-risk first fix.","Q-ARCH should carry target-authority vs recovered-context and readiness-action vs freshness-label as the two scoped seams."],"timestamp":"2026-04-27T17:22:17Z"}
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-002.out:114:**COMMAND-DRIVEN FAN-OUT IS SUPPORTED:** use `--executor`/`--executors`/`--concurrency` flags on `/deep:start-review-loop`. The command's YAML `step_fanout_spawn` owns multi-lineage dispatch; `fanout-merge.cjs` applies strongest-restriction (any lineage active P0 → merged FAIL). This is not ad-hoc shell dispatch — it is the canonical fan-out path. Intra-lineage wave orchestration remains deferred.

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/manual_testing_playbook/09--fanout/fanout-merge-review-strongest-restriction.md:26:- Objective: Confirm `mergeReviewRegistries` applies strongest-restriction: any active P0 across any lineage → `mergedVerdict=FAIL`; duplicate findingId escalates to highest severity; non-active findings excluded; all clean → PASS; P1-only → CONDITIONAL.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/manual_testing_playbook/09--fanout/fanout-merge-review-strongest-restriction.md:29:- Expected signals: clean+P0 → `mergedVerdict=FAIL`, `activeP0=1`; all clean → `mergedVerdict=PASS`, `activeP0=0`; P1-only → `mergedVerdict=CONDITIONAL`; duplicate `findingId` P2+P0 → escalates to P0 → FAIL, single deduplicated finding; `resolved_false_positive` excluded from `activeP0`.

     1	import { createRequire } from 'node:module';
     2	import { existsSync, mkdirSync, mkdtempSync, readFileSync, rmSync, writeFileSync } from 'node:fs';

     4	import { join } from 'node:path';
     5	
     6	import { afterEach, describe, expect, it } from 'vitest';

     8	import { runtimeRoot, spawnCjs } from '../helpers/spawn-cjs';

    10	const require = createRequire(import.meta.url);
    11	const {
    12	  mergeResearchRegistries,
    13	  mergeReviewRegistries,
    14	  buildAttributionMd,
    15	} = require('../../scripts/fanout-merge.cjs') as {
    16	  mergeResearchRegistries: (lineageData: Array<{ label: string; registry: Record<string, unknown> | null }>) => Record<string, unknown>;
    17	  mergeReviewRegistries: (lineageData: Array<{ label: string; registry: Record<string, unknown> | null }>) => Record<string, unknown>;
    18	  buildAttributionMd: (lineageData: unknown[], loopType: string) => string;
    19	};
    20	
    21	const tempDirs: string[] = [];
    22	
    23	function makeTempDir(prefix: string): string {
    24	  const dir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), prefix));
    25	  tempDirs.push(dir);
    26	  return dir;
    27	}
    28	
    29	afterEach(() => {
    30	  while (tempDirs.length > 0) {
    31	    const dir = tempDirs.pop();
    32	    if (dir) rmSync(dir, { recursive: true, force: true });
    33	  }
    34	});
    35	
    36	const fanoutMergeScript = join(runtimeRoot, 'scripts', 'fanout-merge.cjs');
    37	
    38	// ─── Research merge unit tests ─────────────────────────────────────────────
    39	
    40	describe('mergeResearchRegistries', () => {
    41	  it('deduplicates findings with the same id across lineages', () => {
    42	    const data = [
    43	      {
    44	        label: 'a',
    45	        registry: {
    46	          keyFindings: [{ id: 'F1', title: 'Finding one', confidence: 0.9 }],
    47	          openQuestions: [],
    48	          ruledOutDirections: [],
    49	          metrics: { iterationsCompleted: 3, convergenceScore: 0.8, openQuestions: 0, resolvedQuestions: 0, keyFindings: 1, coverageBySources: {} },
    50	        },
    51	      },
    52	      {
    53	        label: 'b',
    54	        registry: {
    55	          keyFindings: [{ id: 'F1', title: 'Finding one (duplicate)', confidence: 0.7 }, { id: 'F2', title: 'Finding two' }],
    56	          openQuestions: [],
    57	          ruledOutDirections: [],
    58	          metrics: { iterationsCompleted: 2, convergenceScore: 0.6, openQuestions: 0, resolvedQuestions: 0, keyFindings: 2, coverageBySources: {} },
    59	        },
    60	      },
    61	    ];
    62	
    63	    const result = mergeResearchRegistries(data);
    64	    const findings = result.keyFindings as Array<{ id: string; _lineages: string[] }>;
    65	
    66	    // F1 deduplicated — only one entry
    67	    expect(findings.filter((f) => f.id === 'F1')).toHaveLength(1);
    68	    // F2 present
    69	    expect(findings.filter((f) => f.id === 'F2')).toHaveLength(1);
    70	    // Total: 2 unique findings
    71	    expect(findings).toHaveLength(2);
    72	
    73	    // F1 should track both lineages
    74	    const f1 = findings.find((f) => f.id === 'F1')!;
    75	    expect(f1._lineages).toContain('a');
    76	    expect(f1._lineages).toContain('b');
    77	  });
    78	
    79	  it('aggregates total iteration count across lineages', () => {
    80	    const data = [
    81	      { label: 'a', registry: { keyFindings: [], openQuestions: [], ruledOutDirections: [], metrics: { iterationsCompleted: 4, convergenceScore: 0.9, openQuestions: 0, resolvedQuestions: 0, keyFindings: 0, coverageBySources: {} } } },
    82	      { label: 'b', registry: { keyFindings: [], openQuestions: [], ruledOutDirections: [], metrics: { iterationsCompleted: 3, convergenceScore: 0.7, openQuestions: 0, resolvedQuestions: 0, keyFindings: 0, coverageBySources: {} } } },
    83	    ];
    84	
    85	    const result = mergeResearchRegistries(data);
    86	    const metrics = result.metrics as { iterationsCompleted: number; convergenceScore: number };
    87	    expect(metrics.iterationsCompleted).toBe(7);
    88	    expect(metrics.convergenceScore).toBeCloseTo(0.8, 2);
    89	  });
    90	
    91	  it('handles lineages with null registry gracefully', () => {
    92	    const data = [
    93	      { label: 'ok', registry: { keyFindings: [{ id: 'F1', title: 'ok' }], openQuestions: [], ruledOutDirections: [], metrics: { iterationsCompleted: 1, convergenceScore: 0.5, openQuestions: 0, resolvedQuestions: 0, keyFindings: 1, coverageBySources: {} } } },
    94	      { label: 'missing', registry: null },
    95	    ];
    96	
    97	    const result = mergeResearchRegistries(data as Parameters<typeof mergeResearchRegistries>[0]);
    98	    expect((result.keyFindings as unknown[]).length).toBe(1);
    99	  });
   100	});
   101	
   102	// ─── Review merge unit tests (strongest-restriction) ──────────────────────
   103	
   104	describe('mergeReviewRegistries — strongest-restriction', () => {
   105	  it('merges FAIL when lineage A is clean but lineage B has an active P0', () => {
   106	    const data = [
   107	      { label: 'clean', registry: { openFindings: [] } },
   108	      { label: 'p0-lineage', registry: { openFindings: [{ findingId: 'F1', severity: 'P0', status: 'active', title: 'Critical bug' }] } },
   109	    ];
   110	
   111	    const result = mergeReviewRegistries(data);
   112	    expect(result.mergedVerdict).toBe('FAIL');
   113	    expect(result.activeP0).toBe(1);
   114	    expect(result.activeP1).toBe(0);
   115	  });
   116	
   117	  it('merges PASS when all lineages have no active P0 or P1', () => {
   118	    const data = [
   119	      { label: 'a', registry: { openFindings: [{ findingId: 'F1', severity: 'P2', status: 'active', title: 'Advisory' }] } },
   120	      { label: 'b', registry: { openFindings: [] } },
   121	    ];
   122	
   123	    const result = mergeReviewRegistries(data);
   124	    expect(result.mergedVerdict).toBe('PASS');
   125	    expect(result.activeP0).toBe(0);
   126	    expect(result.activeP1).toBe(0);
   127	    expect(result.activeP2).toBe(1);
   128	  });
   129	
   130	  it('merges CONDITIONAL when P1 exists but no P0', () => {
   131	    const data = [
   132	      { label: 'a', registry: { openFindings: [{ findingId: 'F1', severity: 'P1', status: 'active', title: 'Non-critical' }] } },
   133	      { label: 'b', registry: { openFindings: [] } },
   134	    ];
   135	
   136	    const result = mergeReviewRegistries(data);
   137	    expect(result.mergedVerdict).toBe('CONDITIONAL');
   138	  });
   139	
   140	  it('escalates to highest severity for duplicate findingId across lineages', () => {
   141	    const data = [
   142	      { label: 'a', registry: { openFindings: [{ findingId: 'F1', severity: 'P2', status: 'active', title: 'X' }] } },
   143	      { label: 'b', registry: { openFindings: [{ findingId: 'F1', severity: 'P0', status: 'active', title: 'X upgraded' }] } },
   144	    ];
   145	
   146	    const result = mergeReviewRegistries(data);
   147	    expect(result.mergedVerdict).toBe('FAIL');
   148	    expect(result.activeP0).toBe(1);
   149	    // Only one deduplicated finding
   150	    expect((result.openFindings as unknown[]).length).toBe(1);
   151	  });
   152	
   153	  it('skips non-active findings (resolved/contested)', () => {
   154	    const data = [
   155	      { label: 'a', registry: { openFindings: [{ findingId: 'F1', severity: 'P0', status: 'resolved_false_positive', title: 'FP' }] } },
   156	    ];
   157	
   158	    const result = mergeReviewRegistries(data);
   159	    expect(result.mergedVerdict).toBe('PASS');
   160	    expect(result.activeP0).toBe(0);
   161	  });
   162	});
   163	
   164	// ─── fanout-merge.cjs script tests ────────────────────────────────────────
   165	
   166	describe('fanout-merge.cjs — script', () => {
   167	  it('exits 0 with ok when no lineages directory exists', async () => {
   168	    const baseDir = makeTempDir('fanout-merge-empty-');
   169	    const result = await spawnCjs(fanoutMergeScript, [
   170	      '--loop-type', 'research',
   171	      '--artifact-dir', baseDir,
   172	    ]);
   173	    expect(result.exitCode).toBe(0);
   174	    const json = JSON.parse(result.stdout.split('\n').filter(Boolean).at(-1) ?? '{}') as Record<string, unknown>;
   175	    expect(json.status).toBe('ok');
   176	  });
   177	
   178	  it('merges two review lineage registries and writes fanout-attribution.md', () => {
   179	    const baseDir = makeTempDir('fanout-merge-review-');
   180	    const lineagesDir = join(baseDir, 'lineages');
   181	
   182	    // Set up lineage A (P0 finding)
   183	    const linADir = join(lineagesDir, 'lina');
   184	    mkdirSync(linADir, { recursive: true });
   185	    writeFileSync(
   186	      join(linADir, 'deep-review-findings-registry.json'),
   187	      JSON.stringify({ openFindings: [{ findingId: 'F1', severity: 'P0', status: 'active', title: 'Critical' }], findingsBySeverity: { P0: 1, P1: 0, P2: 0 }, openFindingsCount: 1, resolvedFindingsCount: 0 }),
   188	      'utf8',
   189	    );
   190	
   191	    // Set up lineage B (clean)
   192	    const linBDir = join(lineagesDir, 'linb');
   193	    mkdirSync(linBDir, { recursive: true });
   194	    writeFileSync(
   195	      join(linBDir, 'deep-review-findings-registry.json'),
   196	      JSON.stringify({ openFindings: [], findingsBySeverity: { P0: 0, P1: 0, P2: 0 }, openFindingsCount: 0, resolvedFindingsCount: 0 }),
   197	      'utf8',
   198	    );
   199	
   200	    // Run merge
   201	    const { execSync } = require('node:child_process');
   202	    execSync(`node "${fanoutMergeScript}" --loop-type review --artifact-dir "${baseDir}"`, { encoding: 'utf8' });
   203	
   204	    // Merged registry should exist at base dir
   205	    const mergedPath = join(baseDir, 'deep-review-findings-registry.json');
   206	    expect(existsSync(mergedPath)).toBe(true);
   207	    const merged = JSON.parse(readFileSync(mergedPath, 'utf8'));
   208	    expect(merged.mergedVerdict).toBe('FAIL');
   209	    expect(merged.activeP0).toBe(1);
   210	
   211	    // Attribution md should exist
   212	    expect(existsSync(join(baseDir, 'fanout-attribution.md'))).toBe(true);
   213	  });
   214	});

.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/references/memory/embedder_architecture.md:200:Persistent document and query embeddings are cached by `content_hash`, active `profile_key`, `input_kind`, `model_id`, and `dimensions`. The profile key is derived from `vec_metadata.active_embedder_provider`, `active_embedder_name`, and `active_embedder_dim`, so switching between Jina, Voyage, OpenAI, or hf-local profiles no longer reuses an incompatible cache row.

.opencode/skills/deep-improvement/references/agent-improvement/candidate_proposal_format.md:29:**Candidate proposal dedup:** `candidate-lineage.json` stores a SHA-256 `contentHash` computed over normalized candidate content after frontmatter and rubric metadata are stripped. The normalized content-hash approach preserves first-seen candidate lineage.
.opencode/skills/deep-improvement/references/agent-improvement/candidate_proposal_format.md:291:contentHash = sha256(normalize(stripFrontmatterAndRubricMetadata(candidateContent)))
.opencode/skills/deep-improvement/references/agent-improvement/candidate_proposal_format.md:294:When a new candidate has the same `contentHash` as a prior node, the lineage graph records it under `duplicates[]` and preserves the first node as the primary proposal.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/references/hooks/skill_advisor_hook.md:201:- Prompt cache keys are HMAC/hash based.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/references/memory/memory_system.md:109:| L2: Core | `memory_save()` | Index a saved continuity artifact or spec doc. Re-generates embedding when **content hash** changes. Title-only changes do not trigger re-embedding. | After generate-context.js |
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/references/memory/memory_system.md:147:| `force` | boolean | false | Force re-index all files (ignore content hash) |
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/references/memory/memory_system.md:150:| `incremental` | boolean | true | Skip files whose mtime and content hash are unchanged since last index |
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/references/memory/memory_system.md:500:The indexed-continuity store uses content hashing and session-aware deduplication to prevent redundant memories and reduce token usage.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/references/memory/memory_system.md:506:Each indexed continuity source is hashed using SHA-256 of its **raw content** (no normalization):
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/references/memory/memory_system.md:514:This hash is stored as `content_hash` in the database and used by `memory_save()` to detect whether a file has changed — if the hash matches, re-embedding is skipped.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/references/memory/memory_system.md:520:For session deduplication, a separate hash identifies individual memories within a session:
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/references/memory/memory_system.md:524:  let hashInput: string;
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/references/memory/memory_system.md:526:  if (memory.content_hash) {
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/references/memory/memory_system.md:527:    hashInput = memory.content_hash;
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/references/memory/memory_system.md:529:    hashInput = `${memory.id}:${memory.anchorId || ''}:${memory.file_path || ''}`;
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/references/memory/memory_system.md:531:    hashInput = JSON.stringify({
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/references/memory/memory_system.md:538:  return crypto.createHash('sha256').update(hashInput).digest('hex').slice(0, 16);
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/references/memory/memory_system.md:542:The hash priority: `content_hash` > `id:anchorId:file_path` > JSON of `{anchor, path, title}`. Truncated to 16 hex chars for storage efficiency.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/references/memory/memory_system.md:549:| Same spec-doc record in same session | 16-char hash match | Skip sending (session dedup) |
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/references/memory/memory_system.md:563:**Note:** There is no content normalization (lowercasing, whitespace/date normalization) in the hashing pipeline. Hashes are computed on raw content, so even whitespace-only changes will produce a different hash and trigger re-indexing.

.opencode/skills/deep-review/README.md:133:Synthesis dedups findings across iterations using content-hash plus file:line, reconciles claim-adjudicated severities, and writes the nine-section `review-report.md`. A convergence-time `resource-map.md` is emitted by default from converged delta evidence. Operators can disable per run with `--no-resource-map`. Auto-resume reads existing state on re-invocation and continues without re-running prior iterations.

.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/state/state_reducer_registry.md:84:1. **PRIMARY: content_hash** = `sha256(file_path + line_range + finding_type + normalized_description_80chars)`
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/state/state_reducer_registry.md:89:2. **FALLBACK (legacy records): file:line + normalized_title** - applied when one or both records lack a `content_hash`, preserving existing behavior unchanged.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/state/state_reducer_registry.md:93:When the same `content_hash` appears across iterations from different dimensions, synthesis collapses them to ONE entry with `dimensions: [<all dimensions that emitted it>]` rather than emitting multiple records. Records without `content_hash` fall back to `file:line + normalized_title`; no migration is required for existing JSONL state.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/references/state/state_reducer_registry.md:97:Every finding emitted into the JSONL delta (`findingDetails[]`) MUST include a `content_hash` field computed per the two-tier match. `reduce-state.cjs` reads this field for synthesis dedup.

.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/changelog/v3.1.2.0.md:158:The duplicate content check queried by content hash without filtering by tenant, user, agent, or shared-space scope. In a multi-tenant database, this could flag a memory as "duplicate" based on another tenant's data. The dry-run response also leaked cross-scope metadata. The duplicate query now threads governed scope into the WHERE clause. Cross-scope metadata is redacted from responses.

.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.3.2.0.md:26:The reducer ignored `finalSeverity` from claim-adjudication packets, so the findings registry used the original severity even after adjudication changed it. The reducer now parses `claim_adjudication` events by `findingId`, uses `finalSeverity` as the main registry value, and seeds transitions from the packet.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/changelog/v3.0.1.0.md:13:The system identifies each memory using a "logical key" built from its spec folder, file path, and anchor. But it didn't account for *who* created the memory. Two users (or two AI agents, or two tenants) working on the same file path would generate identical keys, causing their version histories to collide -- one user's memory would silently overwrite the other's. The logical key now includes a hashed identifier derived from the creator's identity (tenant, user, agent, session, or shared space). Existing memories without identity data keep their old keys for backward compatibility.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/changelog/v3.0.1.0.md:158:The embedding cache looked up entries by content hash and model name, but ignored the vector size. If a model was reconfigured to use different dimensions, the cache would serve stale vectors of the wrong size. Fixed by including dimensions in the cache key.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/changelog/v3.0.1.0.md:297:The incremental scanner compared file modification timestamps, but very fast writes could produce the same timestamp. Fixed by using content-hash comparison as a secondary change detection signal.

.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/changelog/v3.0.1.0.md:365:| `lib/storage/incremental-index.ts` | Content-hash secondary detection |

.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.8.0.0.md:1:This release lands the deep-review portion of the packets 108 plus 110 auto-review uplift program. Four reusable teachings from the upstream `dzianisv/opencode-plugins` auto-review package now apply to every deep-review iteration: a parseable verdict on the final line, a role-banner marker at the rendered-prompt boundary, an anti-repetition rule across iterations, and a content-hash dedup that extends the existing synthesis path. A fifth teaching, H-9 bounded evidence, was deferred to packet 111 plus because current deep-review prompts pass state and metadata pointers rather than embedded file contents, so the council found no real interpolation path to harden. Every mechanism cleared a 5-of-5 unanimous deep-ai-council review by `cli-codex gpt-5.5 xhigh fast` before any code shipped.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.8.0.0.md:27:#### Content-hash dedup extends existing synthesis dedup
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.8.0.0.md:29:The synthesis step already deduplicated findings at `deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:1115-1124` using a file-plus-line plus normalized-title key. That key missed cross-dimension restatements (the same security finding described once under "security" and once under "correctness") and missed minor wording drift across iterations. This release extends the existing key with a content hash: `content_hash = sha256(file_path + line_range + finding_type + normalized_description_80chars)`. Synthesis now matches in two tiers, primary on content_hash, fallback to the legacy file plus line plus normalized title for records that predate this release. Cross-dimension matches collapse to one entry with a `dimensions: [<list>]` field that names every dimension that surfaced the finding. There is no parallel dedup path. The council mandate was to extend, not duplicate. H-7 dedup is intentionally NOT applied to deep-research because deep-research is single-dimension. That decision is documented in the deep-research changelog for the same release window.
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.8.0.0.md:56:| `.opencode/skills/deep-review/SKILL.md` | H-1 final-line verdict contract plus verdict mapping (PASS / CONDITIONAL / FAIL), H-2 marker requirement, H-4 anti-repetition rule under CONSTRAINTS, H-7 content-hash dedup documentation. Three commits: 73e9e361e (H-1), 74782acfb (H-2 plus H-4), 8b3039a1a (H-7). |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.8.0.0.md:58:| `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml` | `step_derive_verdict` added (H-1 enforcement), `step_marker_scan` added (H-2 enforcement), existing synthesis dedup extended with content_hash at lines 1115 through 1124 (H-7). Three commits: 73e9e361e, 74782acfb, 8b3039a1a. |
.opencode/skills/deep-review/changelog/v1.8.0.0.md:59:| `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml` | Mirror of auto, same H-1 verdict derivation, H-2 marker scan, and H-7 content_hash extension. Three commits: 73e9e361e, 74782acfb, 8b3039a1a. |
.opencode/skills/cli-opencode/SKILL.md:298:13. **Destructive-scope-violation prevention (RM-8) for deep-loop dispatches** — If a permissions-matrix config is loaded (via `--permissions-matrix <path>` flag or recipe field), the structured gate applies and the four-layer prose mitigation is bypassed; see `references/permissions-matrix.md`. Without a loaded matrix, any non-interactive `opencode run` with `--dangerously-skip-permissions` against a populated worktree MUST apply the four-layer mitigation as a legacy fallback, deprecated-but-supported during transition: (L1) rendered prompt contains literal `BANNED OPERATIONS` and `ALLOWED WRITE PATHS`; (L2) `--dir` points at a fresh `git worktree`; (L3) main `git status` clean OR committed, recovery-baseline commit hash recorded; (L4) for multi-phase / phase-parent targets, prefer `cli-copilot` + `gpt-5.5 --reasoning-effort high` over `deepseek-v4-pro`. Background: on 2026-05-04 an `opencode-go/deepseek-v4-pro` dispatch under `/deep:start-review-loop:auto` deleted 44 files across two phase folders because the only safeguard was prose and `--dangerously-skip-permissions` granted unrestricted FS write. Full incident + root cause + checklist: `references/destructive_scope_violations.md`.

.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/changelog/v3.2.0.1.md:36:- **Duplicate detection pre-filters by file size** -- Overlap detection was reading up to 20 prior context files and hashing each one. It now checks the 12 most recent candidates and skips any whose file size doesn't match the new content's byte length before doing the full hash comparison. Same correctness, less I/O.

.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/changelog/v2.2.24.0.md:34:There was a reliability problem around Append-only mutation ledger shipped. Append-only mutation ledger shipped: create/update/delete/reindex operations are recorded through SQLite trigger-enforced immutable entries with hash-chain linkage. This matters because the release is clearer and easier to work with.

.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/changelog/v3.0.1.3.md:23:When merging two similar memories, the system reads the original memory, then sends it to an AI service for embedding generation (which involves a network call that can take seconds). After the embedding returns, the merge proceeds using the data it read earlier. But if another save modified or deleted that original memory during the embedding wait, the merge would overwrite the newer version with stale data -- or archive a record that no longer exists. Before committing the merge, the system now reloads the original memory inside the transaction and compares its content hash and last-modified timestamp against what it read before the embedding call. If the original changed, the merge aborts cleanly with a `predecessor_changed` status. If the original was deleted or archived by another process, it aborts with `predecessor_gone`. In both cases, the save falls through to the normal (non-merge) path instead of forcing stale data.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/changelog/v3.0.1.3.md:78:Every time a memory is saved, the system checks three times whether it already exists (by path, by content hash, and by main path). These queries used nullable OR predicates like `(? IS NULL AND col IS NULL) OR col = ?` for optional scope columns (tenant, user, agent, session, shared space). SQLite's query planner can't use indexes with this pattern, so every check scanned the entire table. On a database with thousands of memories, this was the slowest part of every save. The dedup queries are now dynamically assembled to include only the scope columns present in the request as exact-match conditions. The `(main_file_path = ? OR file_path = ?)` pattern was split into two separate probes. Two new partial indexes were added: one for main-path lookups and one for content-hash lookups with scope columns, both filtered to non-chunk rows.

.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/constitutional/verify-before-completion-claims.md:25:Across long sessions, status was repeatedly stated BEFORE it was true — a commit silently blocked by the comment-hygiene pre-commit gate, a strict-validate that was actually FAILED, a doc Edit that silently failed ("string not found") yet was credited in the commit message, a Step-0 boot marked PASS when the daemon had exited with EINVAL, and a fabricated commit hash written into a memory for a commit that never landed. Each was caught only by a later re-check. A false "done" is worse than a slow "done" because downstream steps build on a state that isn't real. (Owner: "Report outcomes faithfully"; "never claim an unverified commit/file.")

.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.11.0.0.md:1:This release lands the deep-research portion of the packets 108 plus 110 auto-review uplift program. Two reusable teachings from the upstream `dzianisv/opencode-plugins` auto-review package now apply to every deep-research iteration: a role-banner marker at the rendered-prompt boundary, and an anti-repetition rule across iterations. The synthesis content-hash dedup that shipped in deep-review v1.8.0.0 is intentionally NOT applied here because deep-research is single-dimension by design and does not produce the cross-dimension restatements that motivated H-7. Both mechanisms cleared a 5-of-5 unanimous deep-ai-council review by `cli-codex gpt-5.5 xhigh fast` before any code shipped.
.opencode/skills/deep-research/changelog/v1.11.0.0.md:23:The synthesis content-hash dedup that landed in deep-review v1.8.0.0 keys partly on a `dimension` field. Deep-research iterations are single-dimension — there is no second-dimension restatement to collapse — so applying the same dedup mechanism would add metadata fields without behavioral payoff. This decision is recorded for cross-reference with the deep-review v1.8.0.0 changelog where the dedup mechanism is documented in full.

.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/templates/examples/level_1/implementation-summary.md:38:Implemented a complete email/password authentication system including user registration, login, and session management. The system uses bcrypt for secure password hashing and JWT tokens for stateless session management. Both registration and login flows are fully functional with appropriate error handling for common edge cases.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/templates/examples/level_1/implementation-summary.md:46:| `src/utils/hash.js` | Created | bcrypt wrapper for password hashing/verification |
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/templates/examples/level_1/implementation-summary.md:77:| Unit | Pass | hash.js functions tested with Jest (100% coverage) |
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/templates/examples/level_3+/decision-record.md:132:User passwords must be securely hashed before storage to protect user data in case of database breach. The hashing algorithm choice affects security strength and server performance (hash computation time). This decision is security-critical and requires Security team approval.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/templates/examples/level_3+/decision-record.md:145:**Summary**: Use bcrypt with a cost factor of 10 for password hashing.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/templates/examples/level_3+/decision-record.md:147:**Details**: The `bcrypt` npm package (v5.1.1) will be used with 10 salt rounds. This provides approximately 100ms hash time on current hardware while maintaining strong security. The cost factor can be increased over time as hardware improves.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/templates/examples/level_3+/decision-record.md:162:**Security Team Input**: Alex (Security Lead) approved bcrypt with 10 rounds as meeting security requirements. Recommended monitoring hash time as hardware improves and increasing cost factor annually.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/templates/examples/level_3+/decision-record.md:171:- Simple API (hash/verify)
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/templates/examples/level_3+/decision-record.md:189:- Registration endpoint (hash on create)
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/templates/examples/level_3+/decision-record.md:195:**Rollback**: N/A - this is a forward-only decision. Existing hashes remain valid; new algorithm would require migration.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/templates/examples/level_3+/spec.md:31:**Key Decisions**: JWT over sessions for stateless auth (ADR-001), bcrypt with 10 rounds for password hashing (ADR-002), localStorage for MVP with HttpOnly migration planned (ADR-003)

.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/templates/examples/level_3+/plan.md:38:This implementation adds a complete email/password authentication system using bcrypt for password hashing and JWT tokens for stateless session management. The architecture follows established patterns in the codebase (MVC) while introducing reusable utilities for hashing and token management. Given the complexity score (82), this plan includes AI execution protocols for multi-agent coordination.

.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/templates/examples/level_2/implementation-summary.md:39:Implemented a complete email/password authentication system including user registration, login, and session management. The system uses bcrypt for secure password hashing, JWT tokens for stateless session management, and express-validator for input validation. Both registration and login flows are fully functional with comprehensive error handling and input validation.

.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/templates/examples/level_3/decision-record.md:124:User passwords must be securely hashed before storage to protect user data in case of database breach. The hashing algorithm choice affects security strength and server performance (hash computation time).
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/templates/examples/level_3/decision-record.md:136:**Summary**: Use bcrypt with a cost factor of 10 for password hashing.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/templates/examples/level_3/decision-record.md:138:**Details**: The `bcrypt` npm package (v5.1.1) will be used with 10 salt rounds. This provides approximately 100ms hash time on current hardware while maintaining strong security. The cost factor can be increased over time as hardware improves.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/templates/examples/level_3/decision-record.md:160:- Simple API (hash/verify)
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/templates/examples/level_3/decision-record.md:178:- Registration endpoint (hash on create)
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/templates/examples/level_3/decision-record.md:182:**Rollback**: N/A - this is a forward-only decision. Existing hashes remain valid; new algorithm would require migration.

.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cache/README.md:16:`lib/cache/` owns two cache surfaces for MCP server retrieval paths. `tool-cache.ts` caches tool results in memory with TTL, key hashing, invalidation and in-flight request coalescing. `embedding-cache.ts` stores content embeddings in SQLite with content-hash keys and LRU eviction.

.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:217:  inputSchema: { type: 'object', additionalProperties: false, properties: { filePath: { type: 'string', minLength: 1, description: 'Absolute path to a spec document under specs/**/ or .opencode/specs/**/ (spec.md, plan.md, tasks.md, checklist.md, decision-record.md, implementation-summary.md, handover.md, research.md, resource-map.md, description.json, graph-metadata.json) or a constitutional memory under .opencode/skills/*/constitutional/' }, force: { type: 'boolean', default: false, description: 'Force re-index even if content hash unchanged' }, dryRun: { type: 'boolean', default: false, description: 'Validate only without saving. Returns validation results including anchor format, duplicate check, and token budget estimation' }, skipPreflight: { type: 'boolean', default: false, description: 'Skip pre-flight validation checks (not recommended)' }, asyncEmbedding: { type: 'boolean', default: false, description: 'When true, embedding generation is deferred for non-blocking saves. The spec-doc record is immediately saved with pending status and an async background attempt is triggered. Default false preserves synchronous embedding behavior.' }, routeAs: { type: 'string', enum: ['narrative_progress', 'narrative_delivery', 'decision', 'handover_state', 'research_finding', 'task_update', 'metadata_only', 'drop'], description: 'Optional routing override hint for canonical continuity saves.' }, mergeModeHint: { type: 'string', enum: ['append-as-paragraph', 'insert-new-adr', 'append-table-row', 'update-in-place', 'append-section'], description: 'Optional merge-mode hint for routed canonical continuity saves.' }, tenantId: { type: 'string', description: 'Tenant boundary for governed ingest.' }, userId: { type: 'string', description: 'User boundary for governed ingest.' }, agentId: { type: 'string', description: 'Agent boundary for governed ingest.' }, sessionId: { type: 'string', description: 'Session boundary for governed ingest.' }, provenanceSource: { type: 'string', description: 'Required provenance source when governed ingest validation applies.' }, provenanceActor: { type: 'string', description: 'Required provenance actor when governed ingest validation applies.' }, governedAt: { type: 'string', description: 'ISO timestamp for governed ingest. Defaults to now when omitted.' }, retentionPolicy: { type: 'string', enum: ['keep', 'ephemeral'], description: 'Retention class applied to the saved spec-doc record.' }, deleteAfter: { type: 'string', description: 'Optional ISO timestamp after which retention sweep may delete the spec-doc record.' } }, required: ['filePath'] },
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:510:  inputSchema: { type: 'object', additionalProperties: false, properties: { specFolder: { type: 'string', description: 'Limit scan to specific spec folder (e.g., "005-memory")' }, force: { type: 'boolean', default: false, description: 'Force re-index all files (ignore content hash)' }, includeConstitutional: { type: 'boolean', default: true, description: 'Whether to scan .opencode/skills/*/constitutional/ directories' }, includeSpecDocs: { type: 'boolean', default: true, description: 'Whether to scan .opencode/specs/ directories for spec folder documents (spec.md, plan.md, tasks.md, checklist.md, decision-record.md, implementation-summary.md, research/research.md, handover.md, resource-map.md). Iteration artifacts under research/iterations/ and review/iterations/ are excluded from spec-doc indexing. Set SPECKIT_INDEX_SPEC_DOCS=false env var to disable globally.' }, incremental: { type: 'boolean', default: true, description: 'Enable incremental indexing. When true (default), skips files whose mtime and content hash are unchanged since last index. Set to false to re-evaluate all files regardless of change detection.' } }, required: [] },

.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/references/graph/skill_graph_drift.md:111:Re-indexes the graph from source plus updates the hash signature.

.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/references/runtime/freshness_contract.md:59:Trust states transition based on three signals: a generation counter, a source-file hash check, plus daemon health.
.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/references/runtime/freshness_contract.md:89:- `live → stale`: daemon detects a hash mismatch on any `.opencode/skills/*/SKILL.md` or `graph-metadata.json` file.
.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/references/runtime/freshness_contract.md:123:- Recomputing the source-hash signature on any watched-file change.
.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/references/runtime/freshness_contract.md:146:| Source-hash regression (rebuild succeeds but state stays stale) | trustState stays `stale` after `advisor_rebuild` | File a bug. The hash computation is broken |

codex
The reducer contract is the interesting bit: the merge script is not the only source of truth, because it has to consume the registries produced by deep-research and deep-review reducers. I’m reading those shapes next, especially their dedup keys.

  1201	function buildRegistry(strategyDimensions, iterationFiles, iterationRecords, config, corruptionWarnings = [], deltaRecords = []) {
  1202	  const terminalStop = buildTerminalStopState(iterationRecords);
  1203	  const lineage = buildLineageState(config, iterationRecords, terminalStop);
  1204	  const { openFindings, resolvedFindings } = buildFindingRegistry(iterationFiles, iterationRecords, deltaRecords);

  1209	  const blockedStopHistory = buildBlockedStopHistory(iterationRecords);

  1214	
  1215	  // Part C REQ-018: split repeatedFindings into two semantically distinct buckets

  1218	    if (finding.lastSeen - finding.firstSeen < 1) return false;
  1219	    const transitions = Array.isArray(finding.transitions) ? finding.transitions : [];
  1220	    // Exclude the initial discovery transition from the "no change" count
  1221	    const nontrivialTransitions = transitions.filter((t) => t.from !== null);
  1222	    return nontrivialTransitions.length === 0;
  1223	  });

  1227	    const nontrivialTransitions = transitions.filter((t) => t.from !== null);
  1228	    return nontrivialTransitions.length > 0;
  1229	  });
  1230	
  1231	  // Deprecated: keep repeatedFindings for backward compatibility with older consumers.

   540	    if (m) {
   541	      fileBase = m[1];
   542	      lineNumber = Number(m[2]);
   543	    } else {
   544	      fileBase = file;
   545	    }
   546	  }
   547	  // Prefer finalSeverity (post-adjudication) over initial severity.
   548	  const rawSeverity = record.finalSeverity || record.severity;
   549	  const severity = normalizeSeverity(rawSeverity);
   550	  if (!severity) {
   551	    return null;
   552	  }
   553	  // Description: claim is most informative; fall back to recommendation/evidence.
   554	  const description = normalizeText(
   555	    record.claim || record.recommendation || record.alternativeExplanation || '',
   556	  );
   557	  return {
   558	    findingId: record.id,
   559	    severity,
   560	    title: normalizeText(record.title || ''),
   561	    file: fileBase,
   562	    line: lineNumber,
   563	    description,
   564	    findingClass: record.findingClass || null,
   565	    deltaStatus: record.status || null,
   566	    // LG-0005: carry the documented findingDetails fields (state_format.md line 226)
   567	    // through to the registry instead of dropping them.
   568	    scopeProof: typeof record.scopeProof === 'string' ? record.scopeProof : null,
   569	    affectedSurfaceHints: asStringList(record.affectedSurfaceHints),
   570	    // LG-0008: carry content_hash so the registry can apply the documented
   571	    // two-tier dedup (SKILL.md 8.1). Null when the record predates the contract.
   572	    contentHash: typeof record.content_hash === 'string' && record.content_hash
   573	      ? record.content_hash
   574	      : (typeof record.contentHash === 'string' && record.contentHash ? record.contentHash : null),
   575	  };
   576	}
   577	
   578	/**
   579	 * LG-0008: two-tier dedup key per SKILL.md 8.1.
   580	 * Primary: content_hash. Fallback (records predating the contract): the legacy
   581	 * `file:line + normalized-title` key. Returns a namespaced string so the two
   582	 * tiers never collide.
   583	 */
   584	function findingDedupKey(finding) {
   585	  if (finding.contentHash) {
   586	    return `ch:${finding.contentHash}`;
   587	  }
   588	  const file = finding.file || '';
   589	  const line = isFiniteNumber(finding.line) ? finding.line : '';
   590	  const title = normalizeText(finding.title || '').toLowerCase().slice(0, 80);
   591	  return `fl:${file}:${line}|${title}`;
   592	}
   593	
   594	function dedupeDimensions(dimensions) {
   595	  const seen = new Set();
   596	  const out = [];
   597	  for (const dimension of dimensions) {
   598	    if (!dimension) continue;
   599	    if (seen.has(dimension)) continue;
   600	    seen.add(dimension);
   601	    out.push(dimension);
   602	  }
   603	  return out;
   604	}
   605	
   606	/**
   607	 * LG-0008: collapse cross-dimension restatements (same content_hash, different
   608	 * findingId) into one canonical entry carrying a merged `dimensions[]` list,
   609	 * matching the synthesis dedup contract in SKILL.md 8.1. Same-findingId entries
   610	 * are already merged upstream by the findingId-keyed map, so this only collapses
   611	 * genuine cross-dimension/cross-id duplicates. Additive: a finding with a unique
   612	 * key keeps its single-element `dimensions` list and an empty `mergedFindingIds`.
   613	 */
   614	function collapseFindingsByDedupKey(findingEntries) {
   615	  const byKey = new Map();
   616	  for (const finding of findingEntries) {
   617	    const key = findingDedupKey(finding);
   618	    const existing = byKey.get(key);
   619	    if (!existing) {
   620	      byKey.set(key, {
   621	        ...finding,
   622	        dimensions: dedupeDimensions([finding.dimension]),
   623	        mergedFindingIds: [],
   624	      });
   625	      continue;
   626	    }
   627	    // Keep the earliest-discovered finding as canonical; fold the duplicate in.
   628	    if (isFiniteNumber(finding.firstSeen) && finding.firstSeen < existing.firstSeen) {
   629	      existing.firstSeen = finding.firstSeen;
   630	    }
   631	    if (isFiniteNumber(finding.lastSeen) && finding.lastSeen > existing.lastSeen) {
   632	      existing.lastSeen = finding.lastSeen;
   633	    }
   634	    existing.dimensions = dedupeDimensions([...existing.dimensions, finding.dimension]);
   635	    if (finding.findingId && finding.findingId !== existing.findingId
   636	      && !existing.mergedFindingIds.includes(finding.findingId)) {
   637	      existing.mergedFindingIds.push(finding.findingId);
   638	    }
   639	    existing.transitions = mergeTransitions(existing.transitions, finding.transitions);
   640	  }
   641	  return [...byKey.values()];
   642	}
   643	
   644	function buildFindingRegistry(iterationFiles, iterationRecords, deltaRecords = []) {
   645	  const findingById = new Map();
   646	  const claimAdjudicationByFinding = buildClaimAdjudicationByFinding(iterationRecords);
   647	
   648	  // P1-026 fix: extract findings from {type:"finding"} delta records as the
   649	  // primary source. Delta records carry structured fields (id, severity,
   650	  // status, file:line, claim, evidenceRefs) authored by the loop runtime.
   651	  // Iteration-markdown parsing remains as fallback for legacy iteration
   652	  // files that don't have corresponding delta records.
   653	  const deltaFindingsByRun = new Map();
   654	  for (const record of deltaRecords) {
   655	    const finding = deltaRecordToFinding(record);
   656	    if (!finding) continue;
   657	    const run = isFiniteNumber(record.iteration) ? record.iteration : 0;
   658	    if (!deltaFindingsByRun.has(run)) {
   659	      deltaFindingsByRun.set(run, []);
   660	    }
   661	    deltaFindingsByRun.get(run).push(finding);
   662	  }
   663	
   664	  // Process deltas in iteration order so transitions reflect chronology.
   665	  // Cross-reference iteration markdown files for focus/dimensionsAddressed
   666	  // metadata (used by deriveDimension); pseudo iteration if no markdown match.
   667	  const iterationByRun = new Map(iterationFiles.map((it) => [it.run, it]));
   668	  const sortedRuns = [...deltaFindingsByRun.keys()].sort((a, b) => a - b);
   669	  for (const run of sortedRuns) {
   670	    const pseudoIteration = iterationByRun.get(run) || { focus: '', run, dimensionsAddressed: [] };
   671	    for (const finding of deltaFindingsByRun.get(run)) {
   672	      const claimAdjudication = claimAdjudicationByFinding.get(finding.findingId);
   673	      const canonicalSeverity = claimAdjudication?.finalSeverity || finding.severity;
   674	      const existing = findingById.get(finding.findingId);
   675	      if (!existing) {
   676	        findingById.set(finding.findingId, {
   677	          ...finding,
   678	          severity: canonicalSeverity,
   679	          dimension: deriveDimension(
   680	            { ...finding, severity: canonicalSeverity },
   681	            pseudoIteration,
   682	          ),
   683	          firstSeen: run,
   684	          lastSeen: run,
   685	          status: 'active',
   686	          transitions: mergeTransitions(
   687	            [{
   688	              iteration: run,
   689	              from: null,
   690	              to: canonicalSeverity,
   691	              reason: 'Initial discovery (delta)',
   692	            }],
   693	            claimAdjudication?.transitions,
   694	          ),
   695	        });
   696	      } else {
   697	        existing.lastSeen = run;
   698	        if (existing.severity !== canonicalSeverity) {
   699	          existing.transitions.push({
   700	            iteration: run,
   701	            from: existing.severity,
   702	            to: canonicalSeverity,
   703	            reason: 'Severity adjusted in later delta',
   704	          });
   705	          existing.severity = canonicalSeverity;
   706	        }
   707	      }
   708	    }
   709	  }
   710	
   711	  for (const iteration of iterationFiles) {
   712	    for (const finding of iteration.findings) {
   713	      const claimAdjudication = claimAdjudicationByFinding.get(finding.findingId);
   714	      const canonicalSeverity = claimAdjudication?.finalSeverity || finding.severity;
   715	      const existing = findingById.get(finding.findingId);
   716	      if (!existing) {
   717	        findingById.set(finding.findingId, {
   718	          ...finding,
   719	          severity: canonicalSeverity,
   720	          dimension: deriveDimension({ ...finding, severity: canonicalSeverity }, iteration),
   721	          firstSeen: iteration.run,
   722	          lastSeen: iteration.run,
   723	          status: 'active',
   724	          transitions: mergeTransitions(
   725	            [{
   726	              iteration: iteration.run,
   727	              from: null,
   728	              to: canonicalSeverity,
   729	              reason: 'Initial discovery',
   730	            }],
   731	            claimAdjudication?.transitions,
   732	          ),
   733	        });
   734	      } else {
   735	        existing.lastSeen = iteration.run;
   736	        if (existing.severity !== canonicalSeverity) {
   737	          existing.transitions.push({
   738	            iteration: iteration.run,
   739	            from: existing.severity,
   740	            to: canonicalSeverity,
   741	            reason: 'Severity adjusted in later iteration',
   742	          });
   743	          existing.severity = canonicalSeverity;
   744	        }
   745	        if (claimAdjudication?.transitions?.length) {
   746	          existing.transitions = mergeTransitions(existing.transitions, claimAdjudication.transitions);
   747	        }
   748	      }
   749	    }
   750	  }
   751	
   752	  const resolvedIdSet = new Set();
   753	  for (const record of iterationRecords) {
   754	    if (Array.isArray(record.resolvedFindings)) {
   755	      for (const id of record.resolvedFindings) {
   756	        resolvedIdSet.add(id);
   757	      }
   758	    }
   759	  }
   760	
   761	  // LG-0008: collapse cross-dimension restatements before splitting open/resolved.
   762	  const collapsedFindings = collapseFindingsByDedupKey([...findingById.values()]);
   763	
   764	  const openFindings = [];
   765	  const resolvedFindings = [];
   766	  for (const finding of collapsedFindings) {
   767	    // A collapsed finding is resolved only when its canonical id and every
   768	    // merged id are resolved, so a duplicate that lingers keeps the entry open.
   769	    const allIds = [finding.findingId, ...(finding.mergedFindingIds || [])];
   770	    const resolved = allIds.length > 0 && allIds.every((id) => resolvedIdSet.has(id));
   771	    if (resolved) {
   772	      finding.status = 'resolved';
   773	      resolvedFindings.push(finding);
   774	    } else {
   775	      openFindings.push(finding);
   776	    }
   777	  }
   778	
   779	  openFindings.sort(compareFindings);
   780	  resolvedFindings.sort(compareFindings);
   781	
   782	  return { openFindings, resolvedFindings };
   783	}
   784	
   785	function compareFindings(left, right) {
   786	  const severityOrder = { P0: 0, P1: 1, P2: 2 };
   787	  const severityDiff = (severityOrder[left.severity] ?? 9) - (severityOrder[right.severity] ?? 9);
   788	  if (severityDiff !== 0) {
   789	    return severityDiff;
   790	  }
