     1	// ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
     2	// ║ Deep Research State Reducer                                              ║
     3	// ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
     4	
     5	'use strict';
     6	
     7	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     8	// 1. IMPORTS
     9	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    10	
    11	const fs = require('node:fs');
    12	const path = require('node:path');
    13	const crypto = require('node:crypto');
    14	const { emitResourceMap } = require('../../system-spec-kit/scripts/resource-map/extract-from-evidence.cjs');
    15	const { resolveArtifactRoot } = require('../../system-spec-kit/shared/review-research-paths.cjs');
    16	
    17	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    18	// 2. HELPERS
    19	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    20	
    21	function readUtf8(filePath) {
    22	  return fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf8');
    23	}
    24	
    25	function writeUtf8(filePath, content) {
    26	  fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(filePath), { recursive: true });
    27	  fs.writeFileSync(filePath, content, 'utf8');
    28	}
    29	
    30	function readJson(filePath) {
    31	  return JSON.parse(readUtf8(filePath));
    32	}
    33	
    34	function getResourceMapEmitSetting(config) {
    35	  const nested = config && typeof config === 'object'
    36	    ? (config.resource_map || config.resourceMap || null)
    37	    : null;
    38	  if (nested && typeof nested === 'object' && typeof nested.emit === 'boolean') {
    39	    return nested.emit;
    40	  }
    41	  return true;
    42	}
    43	
    44	function slugify(value) {
    45	  return value
    46	    .toLowerCase()
    47	    .replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, '-')
    48	    .replace(/^-+|-+$/g, '')
    49	    .slice(0, 80) || 'entry';
    50	}
    51	
    52	function normalizeText(value) {
    53	  return value.replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim();
    54	}
    55	
    56	function contentHash(value) {
    57	  return crypto
    58	    .createHash('sha256')
    59	    .update(normalizeText(String(value)).toLowerCase())
    60	    .digest('hex')
    61	    .slice(0, 12);
    62	}
    63	
    64	function escapeRegExp(value) {
    65	  return value.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g, '\\$&');
    66	}
    67	
    68	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    69	// 3. PARSERS
    70	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    71	
    72	/**
    73	 * Parse JSONL content into an array of records. Backward-compatible array
    74	 * return; corrupt lines are silently dropped. For fail-closed behavior use
    75	 * parseJsonlDetailed().
    76	 *
    77	 * @param {string} jsonlContent - Newline-delimited JSON string
    78	 * @returns {Array<Object>} Parsed records
    79	 */
    80	function parseJsonl(jsonlContent) {
    81	  return parseJsonlDetailed(jsonlContent).records;
    82	}
    83	
    84	/**
    85	 * Parse JSONL content and report malformed lines (fail-closed pathway,
    86	 * research reducer parity with deep-review Part C).
    87	 *
    88	 * The reducer exit code is non-zero when corruption warnings are present
    89	 * unless `--lenient` is passed to the CLI (or `lenient:true` to
    90	 * reduceResearchState).
    91	 *
    92	 * @param {string} jsonlContent - Newline-delimited JSON string
    93	 * @returns {{records: Array<Object>, corruptionWarnings: Array<{line: number, raw: string, error: string}>}}
    94	 */
    95	function parseJsonlDetailed(jsonlContent) {
    96	  const records = [];
    97	  const corruptionWarnings = [];
    98	  let lineNumber = 0;
    99	
   100	  for (const rawLine of jsonlContent.split('\n')) {
   101	    lineNumber += 1;
   102	    const line = rawLine.trim();
   103	    if (!line) {
   104	      continue;
   105	    }
   106	
   107	    try {
   108	      records.push(JSON.parse(line));
   109	    } catch (error) {
   110	      corruptionWarnings.push({
   111	        line: lineNumber,
   112	        raw: rawLine.length > 200 ? `${rawLine.slice(0, 200)}...` : rawLine,
   113	        error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),
   114	      });
   115	    }
   116	  }
   117	
   118	  return { records, corruptionWarnings };
   119	}
   120	
   121	function loadDeltaPayloads(deltaDir) {
   122	  if (!fs.existsSync(deltaDir)) {
   123	    return [];
   124	  }
   125	
   126	  return fs.readdirSync(deltaDir)
   127	    .filter((fileName) => /^iter-\d+\.jsonl$/.test(fileName))
   128	    .sort((left, right) => left.localeCompare(right, undefined, { numeric: true }))
   129	    .map((fileName) => {
   130	      const { records, corruptionWarnings } = parseJsonlDetailed(readUtf8(path.join(deltaDir, fileName)));
   131	      if (!corruptionWarnings.length) {
   132	        return records;
   133	      }
   134	      console.warn(
   135	        `[deep-research] resource-map extractor skipped ${corruptionWarnings.length} malformed delta row(s) from ${fileName}`,
   136	      );
   137	      return records.concat(new Array(corruptionWarnings.length).fill(null));
   138	    });
   139	}
   140	
   141	function createCorruptionError(stateLogPath, corruptionWarnings) {
   142	  const preview = corruptionWarnings
   143	    .slice(0, 3)
   144	    .map((warning) => `  - line ${warning.line}: ${warning.error}`)
   145	    .join('\n');
   146	  const error = new Error(
   147	    `[deep-research] parseJsonl detected ${corruptionWarnings.length} corrupt line(s) in ${stateLogPath}:\n${preview}\n`
   148	    + 'Pass --lenient to the reducer CLI (or lenient:true to reduceResearchState) to ignore corruption.',
   149	  );
   150	  error.code = 'STATE_CORRUPTION';
   151	  error.corruptionWarnings = corruptionWarnings;
   152	  error.stateLogPath = stateLogPath;
   153	  return error;
   154	}

   156	function extractSection(markdown, heading) {
   157	  // Drop the `m` flag so `$` anchors to end-of-string, not end-of-line
   158	  const pattern = new RegExp(`(?:^|\\n)##\\s+${escapeRegExp(heading)}\\s*\\n([\\s\\S]*?)(?=\\n##\\s|$)`, 'i');
   159	  const match = markdown.match(pattern);
   160	  return match ? match[1].trim() : '';
   161	}
   162	
   163	function extractListItems(sectionText) {
   164	  return sectionText
   165	    .split('\n')
   166	    .map((line) => line.trim())
   167	    .filter((line) => /^([-*]|\d+\.)\s+/.test(line))
   168	    .map((line) => normalizeText(line.replace(/^([-*]|\d+\.)\s+/, '')))
   169	    .filter(Boolean);
   170	}
   171	
   172	function parseReflectionValue(sectionText, label) {
   173	  const match = sectionText.match(new RegExp(`-\\s+${escapeRegExp(label)}\\s*:\\s*(.+)`, 'i'));
   174	  return match ? normalizeText(match[1]) : null;
   175	}
   176	
   177	/**
   178	 * Parse a single iteration markdown file into a structured record.
   179	 *
   180	 * @param {string} iterationPath - Absolute path to an iteration-NNN.md file
   181	 * @returns {Object} Parsed iteration with focus, findings, sources, and reflection fields
   182	 */
   183	function parseIterationFile(iterationPath) {
   184	  const markdown = readUtf8(iterationPath);
   185	  const runMatch = iterationPath.match(/iteration-(\d+)\.md$/);
   186	  const headingMatch = markdown.match(/^#\s+Iteration\s+\d+:\s+(.+)$/m);
   187	  const focusSection = extractSection(markdown, 'Focus');
   188	  const findingsSection = extractSection(markdown, 'Findings');
   189	  const ruledOutSection = extractSection(markdown, 'Ruled Out');
   190	  const deadEndsSection = extractSection(markdown, 'Dead Ends');
   191	  const sourcesSection = extractSection(markdown, 'Sources Consulted');
   192	  const reflectionSection = extractSection(markdown, 'Reflection');
   193	  const nextFocusSection = extractSection(markdown, 'Recommended Next Focus');
   194	
   195	  return {
   196	    path: iterationPath,
   197	    run: runMatch ? Number(runMatch[1]) : 0,
   198	    focus: normalizeText(focusSection || (headingMatch ? headingMatch[1] : 'Unknown focus')),
   199	    findings: extractListItems(findingsSection),
   200	    ruledOut: extractListItems(ruledOutSection),
   201	    deadEnds: extractListItems(deadEndsSection),
   202	    sources: extractListItems(sourcesSection),
   203	    reflectionWorked: parseReflectionValue(reflectionSection, 'What worked and why'),
   204	    reflectionFailed: parseReflectionValue(reflectionSection, 'What did not work and why'),
   205	    reflectionDifferent: parseReflectionValue(reflectionSection, 'What I would do differently'),
   206	    nextFocus: normalizeText(nextFocusSection),
   207	  };
   208	}
   209	
   210	function parseStrategyQuestions(strategyContent) {
   211	  const section = extractSection(strategyContent, '3. KEY QUESTIONS (remaining)');
   212	  return section
   213	    .split('\n')
   214	    .map((line) => line.trim())
   215	    .filter((line) => /^- \[[ xX]\]\s+/.test(line))
   216	    .map((line) => {
   217	      const checked = /^- \[[xX]\]\s+/.test(line);
   218	      const text = normalizeText(line.replace(/^- \[[ xX]\]\s+/, ''));
   219	      return {
   220	        checked,
   221	        text,
   222	      };
   223	    });
   224	}
   225	
   226	function buildCoverageBySources(iterationFiles, iterationRecords) {
   227	  const counter = {};
   228	  const seen = new Set();
   229	
   230	  for (const source of iterationFiles.flatMap((iteration) => iteration.sources).concat(
   231	    iterationRecords.flatMap((record) => (Array.isArray(record.sourcesQueried) ? record.sourcesQueried : [])),
   232	  )) {
   233	    const normalized = normalizeText(source);
   234	    if (!normalized || seen.has(normalized)) {
   235	      continue;
   236	    }
   237	    seen.add(normalized);
   238	
   239	    let key = 'other';
   240	    if (/^https?:\/\//i.test(normalized)) {
   241	      try {
   242	        key = new URL(normalized).hostname;
   243	      } catch (_error) {
   244	        key = 'web';
   245	      }
   246	    } else if (normalized.startsWith('memory:')) {
   247	      key = 'memory';
   248	    } else if (normalized.includes(':')) {
   249	      key = 'code';
   250	    }
   251	
   252	    counter[key] = (counter[key] || 0) + 1;
   253	  }
   254	
   255	  return Object.fromEntries(Object.entries(counter).sort(([left], [right]) => left.localeCompare(right)));
   256	}

   258	function uniqueById(items) {
   259	  const seen = new Set();
   260	  const result = [];

 succeeded in 0ms:
   260	  const result = [];
   261	
   262	  for (const item of items) {
   263	    if (seen.has(item.id)) {
   264	      continue;
   265	    }
   266	    seen.add(item.id);
   267	    result.push(item);
   268	  }
   269	
   270	  return result;
   271	}
   272	
   273	function uniqueRuledOutByContent(items) {
   274	  const seen = new Set();
   275	  const result = [];
   276	
   277	  for (const item of items) {
   278	    const hash = item.contentHash || contentHash(item.text);
   279	    if (seen.has(hash)) {
   280	      continue;
   281	    }
   282	    seen.add(hash);
   283	    result.push({
   284	      ...item,
   285	      contentHash: hash,
   286	    });
   287	  }
   288	
   289	  return result;
   290	}

   292	function isFiniteNumber(value) {
   293	  return typeof value === 'number' && Number.isFinite(value);
   294	}
   295	
   296	function computeGraphConvergenceScore(signals) {
   297	  if (!signals || typeof signals !== 'object' || Array.isArray(signals)) {
   298	    return 0;
   299	  }
   300	
   301	  const namedScore = signals.blendedScore
   302	    ?? signals.score
   303	    ?? signals.convergenceScore
   304	    ?? signals.compositeScore
   305	    ?? signals.stopScore
   306	    ?? signals.decisionScore;
   307	  if (isFiniteNumber(namedScore)) {
   308	    return namedScore;
   309	  }
   310	
   311	  const numericSignals = Object.values(signals)
   312	    .filter((value) => isFiniteNumber(value));
   313	  if (!numericSignals.length) {
   314	    return 0;
   315	  }
   316	
   317	  const sum = numericSignals.reduce((total, value) => total + value, 0);
   318	  return sum / numericSignals.length;
   319	}
   320	
   321	function buildGraphConvergenceRollup(eventRecords) {
   322	  const latestGraphConvergence = eventRecords.filter((record) => record.event === 'graph_convergence').at(-1);
   323	  const signals = latestGraphConvergence?.signals && typeof latestGraphConvergence.signals === 'object'
   324	    ? latestGraphConvergence.signals
   325	    : {};
   326	  const blockers = Array.isArray(latestGraphConvergence?.blockers)
   327	    ? latestGraphConvergence.blockers
   328	    : [];
   329	
   330	  return {
   331	    graphConvergenceScore: computeGraphConvergenceScore(signals),
   332	    graphDecision: typeof latestGraphConvergence?.decision === 'string'
   333	      ? latestGraphConvergence.decision
   334	      : null,
   335	    graphBlockers: blockers,
   336	  };
   337	}
   338	
   339	function buildLineageState(config, eventRecords) {
   340	  const configLineage = config.lineage && typeof config.lineage === 'object'
   341	    ? config.lineage

   344	    .filter((record) => record.event === 'resumed' || record.event === 'restarted')
   345	    .at(-1);
   346	  const eventContinuedFromRun = latestLifecycleEvent
   347	    ? latestLifecycleEvent.continuedFromRun ?? latestLifecycleEvent.fromIteration
   348	    : undefined;
   349	  const eventHasParentSession = latestLifecycleEvent
   350	    && Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(latestLifecycleEvent, 'parentSessionId');
   351	
   352	  return {
   353	    sessionId: typeof latestLifecycleEvent?.sessionId === 'string' && latestLifecycleEvent.sessionId
   354	      ? latestLifecycleEvent.sessionId
   355	      : typeof configLineage.sessionId === 'string' && configLineage.sessionId
   356	        ? configLineage.sessionId
   357	        : null,
   358	    parentSessionId: eventHasParentSession
   359	      ? (typeof latestLifecycleEvent.parentSessionId === 'string' && latestLifecycleEvent.parentSessionId
   360	          ? latestLifecycleEvent.parentSessionId
   361	          : null)
   362	      : typeof configLineage.parentSessionId === 'string' && configLineage.parentSessionId
   363	        ? configLineage.parentSessionId
   364	        : null,
   365	    lineageMode: typeof latestLifecycleEvent?.lineageMode === 'string' && latestLifecycleEvent.lineageMode
   366	      ? latestLifecycleEvent.lineageMode
   367	      : typeof configLineage.lineageMode === 'string' && configLineage.lineageMode
   368	        ? configLineage.lineageMode
   369	        : 'new',
   370	    generation: isFiniteNumber(latestLifecycleEvent?.generation)
   371	      ? latestLifecycleEvent.generation
   372	      : isFiniteNumber(configLineage.generation)
   373	        ? configLineage.generation
   374	        : 1,
   375	    continuedFromRun: eventContinuedFromRun !== undefined
   376	      ? (isFiniteNumber(eventContinuedFromRun) ? eventContinuedFromRun : null)
   377	      : isFiniteNumber(configLineage.continuedFromRun)
   378	        ? configLineage.continuedFromRun

   383	function buildTerminalStopState(records) {
   384	  const eventRecords = records.filter((record) => record?.type === 'event');
   385	  const latestSynthesisComplete = eventRecords.filter((record) => record.event === 'synthesis_complete').at(-1);
   386	  if (!latestSynthesisComplete) {
   387	    return null;
   388	  }
   389	
   390	  const latestSynthesisIndex = records.lastIndexOf(latestSynthesisComplete);
   391	  const latestIterationIndex = Math.max(
   392	    records.findLastIndex((record) => record?.type === 'iteration'),
   393	    records.findLastIndex((record) => record?.type === 'event' && (record?.event === 'resumed' || record?.event === 'restarted')),
   394	  );
   395	  if (latestSynthesisIndex < latestIterationIndex) {
   396	    return null;
   397	  }
   398	
   399	  return {
   400	    stopReason: typeof latestSynthesisComplete.stopReason === 'string' && latestSynthesisComplete.stopReason
   401	      ? latestSynthesisComplete.stopReason
   402	      : null,
   403	    totalIterations: isFiniteNumber(latestSynthesisComplete.totalIterations)
   404	      ? latestSynthesisComplete.totalIterations
   405	      : null,
   406	    answeredCount: isFiniteNumber(latestSynthesisComplete.answeredCount)
   407	      ? latestSynthesisComplete.answeredCount
   408	      : null,
   409	    totalQuestions: isFiniteNumber(latestSynthesisComplete.totalQuestions)
   410	      ? latestSynthesisComplete.totalQuestions
   411	      : null,
   412	    timestamp: typeof latestSynthesisComplete.timestamp === 'string' && latestSynthesisComplete.timestamp
   413	      ? latestSynthesisComplete.timestamp
   414	      : null,
   415	  };
   416	}

   418	function deriveDashboardStatus(config, iterationRecords, eventRecords, terminalStop) {
   419	  if (terminalStop) {
   420	    return 'COMPLETE';
   421	  }
   422	
   423	  const latestStuckRecovery = eventRecords.filter((record) => record.event === 'stuckRecovery').at(-1);
   424	  const latestIterationTimestamp = getTimestampValue(iterationRecords.at(-1)?.timestamp);
   425	  const latestRecoveryTimestamp = getTimestampValue(latestStuckRecovery?.timestamp);
   426	  if (latestRecoveryTimestamp >= latestIterationTimestamp && latestRecoveryTimestamp > Number.NEGATIVE_INFINITY) {
   427	    return 'STUCK_RECOVERY';
   428	  }
   429	
   430	  if (iterationRecords.at(-1)?.status === 'stuck') {
   431	    return 'STUCK_RECOVERY';
   432	  }
   433	
   434	  const rawStatus = String(config.status || 'initialized').toLowerCase();
   435	  if (rawStatus === 'running') {
   436	    return 'ITERATING';
   437	  }
   438	  if (rawStatus === 'complete' || rawStatus === 'completed') {
   439	    return 'COMPLETE';
   440	  }
   441	  return rawStatus.toUpperCase();
   442	}

   444	function formatBlockedByList(blockedBy) {
   445	  return Array.isArray(blockedBy) && blockedBy.length
   446	    ? blockedBy.join(', ')
   447	    : 'unspecified gates';
   448	}
   449	
   450	function formatSummaryValue(value) {
   451	  if (Array.isArray(value)) {
   452	    return value.map((entry) => formatSummaryValue(entry)).join(', ');
   453	  }
   454	
   455	  if (value && typeof value === 'object') {
   456	    return Object.keys(value)
   457	      .sort()
   458	      .map((key) => `${key}=${formatSummaryValue(value[key])}`)
   459	      .join(', ');
   460	  }
   461	
   462	  return String(value);
   463	}
   464	
   465	function summarizeGateResults(gateResults) {
   466	  if (!gateResults || typeof gateResults !== 'object' || !Object.keys(gateResults).length) {
   467	    return 'none recorded';
   468	  }
   469	
   470	  return Object.keys(gateResults)
   471	    .sort()
   472	    .map((gateName) => {
   473	      const result = gateResults[gateName];
   474	      if (!result || typeof result !== 'object') {
   475	        return `${gateName}=${formatSummaryValue(result)}`;
   476	      }
   477	
   478	      const status = typeof result.pass === 'boolean'
   479	        ? (result.pass ? 'pass' : 'fail')
   480	        : 'unknown';
   481	      const details = Object.keys(result)
   482	        .filter((key) => key !== 'pass' && result[key] !== undefined && result[key] !== null && result[key] !== '')
   483	        .sort()
   484	        .map((key) => `${key}=${formatSummaryValue(result[key])}`);
   485	
   486	      return details.length
   487	        ? `${gateName}=${status} (${details.join(', ')})`
   488	        : `${gateName}=${status}`;
   489	    })
   490	    .join('; ');
   491	}
   492	
   493	function formatGraphBlocker(blocker) {
   494	  if (!blocker || typeof blocker !== 'object') {
   495	    return formatSummaryValue(blocker);
   496	  }
   497	
   498	  const name = typeof blocker.name === 'string' && blocker.name ? blocker.name : 'unnamed-blocker';
   499	  const severity = typeof blocker.severity === 'string' && blocker.severity ? ` (${blocker.severity})` : '';
   500	  const detail = typeof blocker.detail === 'string' && blocker.detail ? blocker.detail : null;
   501	
   502	  if (detail) {
   503	    return `${name}${severity}: ${detail}`;
   504	  }
   505	
   506	  const extras = Object.keys(blocker)
   507	    .filter((key) => !['name', 'severity', 'detail'].includes(key))
   508	    .sort()
   509	    .map((key) => `${key}=${formatSummaryValue(blocker[key])}`);
   510	
   511	  return extras.length ? `${name}${severity}: ${extras.join(', ')}` : `${name}${severity}`;
   512	}
   513	
   514	function getTimestampValue(timestamp) {
   515	  const value = Date.parse(timestamp);
   516	  return Number.isFinite(value) ? value : Number.NEGATIVE_INFINITY;
   517	}

   519	function resolveNextFocus(registry, iterationFiles, iterationRecords) {

   523	  const latestIteration = Array.isArray(iterationRecords) ? iterationRecords.at(-1) : null;
   524	
   525	  if (latestBlockedStop) {
   526	    const latestBlockedTimestamp = getTimestampValue(latestBlockedStop.timestamp);
   527	    const latestIterationTimestamp = getTimestampValue(latestIteration?.timestamp);
   528	
   529	    if (latestBlockedTimestamp > latestIterationTimestamp) {
   530	      return [
   531	        `BLOCKED on: ${formatBlockedByList(latestBlockedStop.blockedBy)}`,
   532	        `Recovery: ${latestBlockedStop.recoveryStrategy || '[No recovery strategy recorded]'}`,
   533	        'Address the blocking gates before the next iteration.',
   534	      ].join('\n');
   535	    }
   536	  }
   537	
   538	  return iterationFiles.map((iteration) => iteration.nextFocus).filter(Boolean).at(-1)
   539	    || registry.openQuestions[0]?.text
   540	    || '[All tracked questions are resolved]';
   541	}

   627	    continuedFromRun: lineage.continuedFromRun ?? null,
   628	    terminalStop: reducerState.terminalStop || null,
   629	    openQuestions: keyedQuestions.filter((question) => !question.resolved).map((question) => ({
   630	      id: question.id,
   631	      text: question.text,
   632	      addedAtIteration: question.addedAtIteration,
   633	      resolvedAtIteration: null,
   634	    })),
   635	    resolvedQuestions: keyedQuestions.filter((question) => question.resolved).map((question) => ({
   636	      id: question.id,
   637	      text: question.text,
   638	      addedAtIteration: question.addedAtIteration,
   639	      resolvedAtIteration: question.resolvedAtIteration,
   640	    })),
   641	    uncoveredQuestions,
   642	    keyFindings,
   643	    ruledOutDirections,
   644	    blockedStopHistory,
   645	    graphConvergenceScore: graphConvergence.graphConvergenceScore,
   646	    graphDecision: graphConvergence.graphDecision,
   647	    graphBlockers: graphConvergence.graphBlockers,
   648	    metrics: {
   649	      iterationsCompleted: iterationRecords.filter((record) => record.type === 'iteration').length,
   650	      openQuestions: keyedQuestions.filter((question) => !question.resolved).length,
   651	      resolvedQuestions: keyedQuestions.filter((question) => question.resolved).length,
   652	      keyFindings: keyFindings.length,
   653	      convergenceScore,
   654	      coverageBySources,
   655	    },
   656	  };
   657	}
   658	
   659	function blockFromBulletList(items) {
   660	  if (!items.length) {
   661	    return '[None yet]';
   662	  }
   663	
   664	  return items.map((item) => `- ${item}`).join('\n');
   665	}
   666	
   667	function buildExhaustedApproaches(iterationFiles) {
   668	  const grouped = new Map();
   669	
   670	  for (const iteration of iterationFiles) {
   671	    for (const entry of iteration.deadEnds.concat(iteration.ruledOut)) {
   672	      const key = entry;
   673	      const bucket = grouped.get(key) || [];
   674	      bucket.push(iteration.run);
   675	      grouped.set(key, bucket);
   676	    }
   677	  }
   678	
   679	  if (!grouped.size) {
   680	    return '[No exhausted approach categories yet]';
   681	  }
   682	
   683	  const blocked = Array.from(grouped.entries())
   684	    .sort(([left], [right]) => left.localeCompare(right))
   685	    .map(([entry, runs]) => {
   686	      const attemptCount = runs.length;
   687	      const lastRun = Math.max(...runs);
   688	      return [
   689	        `### ${entry} -- BLOCKED (iteration ${lastRun}, ${attemptCount} attempts)`,
   690	        `- What was tried: ${entry}`,
   691	        `- Why blocked: Repeated iteration evidence ruled this direction out.`,
   692	        `- Do NOT retry: ${entry}`,
   693	      ].join('\n');
   694	    });
   695	
   696	  return blocked.join('\n\n');
   697	}
   698	
   699	function replaceAnchorSection(content, anchorId, heading, body) {
   700	  const pattern = new RegExp(`<!-- ANCHOR:${anchorId} -->[\\s\\S]*?<!-- \\/ANCHOR:${anchorId} -->`, 'm');
   701	  const replacement = [
   702	    `<!-- ANCHOR:${anchorId} -->`,
   703	    `## ${heading}`,
   704	    body.trim() ? body.trim() : '[None yet]',
   705	    '',
   706	    `<!-- /ANCHOR:${anchorId} -->`,
   707	  ].join('\n');
   708	
   709	  if (!pattern.test(content)) {
   710	    throw new Error(`Missing anchor section ${anchorId} in strategy file`);
   711	  }
   712	
   713	  return content.replace(pattern, replacement);
   714	}
   715	
   716	function updateStrategyContent(strategyContent, registry, iterationFiles, iterationRecords) {
   717	  const answeredTexts = registry.resolvedQuestions.map((question) => question.text);
   718	  const questionEntries = parseStrategyQuestions(strategyContent);
   719	  const answeredSet = new Set(answeredTexts.map(normalizeText));
   720	  const rewrittenQuestionLines = questionEntries.map((question) => {
   721	    const checked = answeredSet.has(normalizeText(question.text));
   722	    return `- [${checked ? 'x' : ' '}] ${question.text}`;
   723	  });
   724	
   725	  const whatWorked = iterationFiles
   726	    .filter((iteration) => iteration.reflectionWorked)
   727	    .map((iteration) => `${iteration.reflectionWorked} (iteration ${iteration.run})`);
   728	  const whatFailed = iterationFiles
   729	    .filter((iteration) => iteration.reflectionFailed)
   730	    .map((iteration) => `${iteration.reflectionFailed} (iteration ${iteration.run})`);
   731	  const nextFocus = resolveNextFocus(registry, iterationFiles, iterationRecords);
   732	
   733	  let updated = strategyContent;
   734	  updated = replaceAnchorSection(updated, 'key-questions', '3. KEY QUESTIONS (remaining)', rewrittenQuestionLines.join('\n'));
   735	  updated = replaceAnchorSection(updated, 'answered-questions', '6. ANSWERED QUESTIONS', blockFromBulletList(answeredTexts));
   736	  updated = replaceAnchorSection(updated, 'what-worked', '7. WHAT WORKED', blockFromBulletList(whatWorked));
   737	  updated = replaceAnchorSection(updated, 'what-failed', '8. WHAT FAILED', blockFromBulletList(whatFailed));
   738	  updated = replaceAnchorSection(updated, 'exhausted-approaches', '9. EXHAUSTED APPROACHES (do not retry)', buildExhaustedApproaches(iterationFiles));
   739	  updated = replaceAnchorSection(
   740	    updated,
   741	    'ruled-out-directions',
   742	    '10. RULED OUT DIRECTIONS',
   743	    blockFromBulletList(registry.ruledOutDirections.map((entry) => `${entry.text} (iteration ${entry.addedAtIteration})`)),
   744	  );
   745	  updated = replaceAnchorSection(updated, 'next-focus', '11. NEXT FOCUS', nextFocus);
   746	  return updated;
   747	}
   748	
   749	function renderDashboard(config, registry, iterationRecords, iterationFiles) {
   750	  const latestIteration = iterationRecords.at(-1);
   751	  const terminalStop = registry.terminalStop && typeof registry.terminalStop === 'object'
   752	    ? registry.terminalStop
   753	    : null;
   754	  const sessionId = registry.sessionId || config.lineage?.sessionId || '[Unknown session]';
   755	  const parentSessionId = registry.parentSessionId ?? config.lineage?.parentSessionId ?? 'none';
   756	  const lineageMode = registry.lineageMode || config.lineage?.lineageMode || 'new';
   757	  const generation = registry.generation ?? config.lineage?.generation ?? 1;
   758	  const continuedFromRun = registry.continuedFromRun ?? config.lineage?.continuedFromRun ?? 'none';
   759	  // Exclude "thought" iterations from rolling average — they are analytical-only
   760	  // and produce no evidence, so including them would artificially lower the ratio.

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	---
     2	title: "Deep Review State Reducer And Registry"
     3	description: "Focused reference for reducer ownership, findings registry semantics, fail-closed behavior, and reconstruction rules."
     4	trigger_phrases:
     5	  - "deep review reducer registry"
     6	  - "review findings registry"
     7	  - "deep review reducer ownership"
     8	  - "review state reconstruction"
     9	---
    10	
    11	# Deep Review State Reducer And Registry
    12	
    13	The reducer is the single writer for derived review state. Iteration agents append evidence; the reducer reconciles it into registry, dashboard, and strategy views.
    14	
    15	---
    16	
    17	## 1. OVERVIEW
    18	
    19	The reducer turns append-only review evidence into the current findings registry, dashboard, and strategy state. It is the authority for derived outputs, not a second source of truth.
    20	
    21	---
    22	
    23	## 2. OWNERSHIP
    24	
    25	| Surface | Writer | Rule |
    26	|---------|--------|------|
    27	| JSONL state | Workflow plus validated iteration append | Append-only |
    28	| Findings registry | `scripts/reduce-state.cjs` | Regenerate from JSONL |
    29	| Dashboard | `scripts/reduce-state.cjs` | Regenerate from registry and latest events |
    30	| Strategy machine sections | `scripts/reduce-state.cjs` | Update from coverage and findings |
    31	| Iteration markdown | Review iteration agent | Write-once |
    32	| Review report | Synthesis | Final handoff |
    33	
    34	No agent should hand-edit reducer-owned derived state as the authoritative fix. Repair JSONL or rerun the reducer.
    35	
    36	---
    37	
    38	## 3. FINDINGS REGISTRY
    39	
    40	The registry groups findings into:
    41	
    42	- Active findings.
    43	- Resolved findings.
    44	- Repeated findings.
    45	- Blocked or disputed findings.
    46	- Claim-adjudicated severity changes.
    47	
    48	Each active finding needs severity, category, file:line evidence, finding class, content hash, first-seen iteration, and latest-seen iteration.
    49	
    50	---
    51	
    52	## 4. FAIL-CLOSED BEHAVIOR
    53	
    54	The reducer must refuse to publish derived state when JSONL is corrupt in strict mode. That protects dashboards and verdict previews from silently losing findings.
    55	
    56	Recovery order:
    57	
    58	1. Identify the malformed JSONL line.
    59	2. Repair or remove only the invalid appended record with an audit note.
    60	3. Re-run the reducer.
    61	4. Confirm registry and dashboard regenerate from the repaired log.
    62	
    63	---
    64	
    65	## 5. RECONSTRUCTION
    66	
    67	When derived files are missing but JSONL and iteration markdown exist:
    68	
    69	1. Rebuild the registry from JSONL finding details.
    70	2. Rebuild dashboard metrics from registry and latest convergence events.
    71	3. Rebuild strategy from completed dimensions and active findings.
    72	4. Re-run synthesis only after reducer outputs are consistent.
    73	
    74	If JSONL is incomplete but iteration files exist, recovery is manual and must be recorded before release readiness is claimed.
    75	
    76	---
    77	
    78	## 6. FINDING DEDUPLICATION
    79	
    80	The reducer deduplicates findings at synthesis time using a two-tier match.
    81	
    82	### Two-Tier Match
    83	
    84	1. **PRIMARY: content_hash** = `sha256(file_path + line_range + finding_type + normalized_description_80chars)`
    85	   - `file_path`: repo-relative path of the finding
    86	   - `line_range`: e.g., `"42"` or `"42-56"`
    87	   - `finding_type`: one of `security`, `correctness`, `performance`, `maintainability`, `test_quality`, `contract_safety`, `removal`
    88	   - `normalized_description_80chars`: first 80 characters of the description, whitespace-collapsed and lowercased
    89	2. **FALLBACK (legacy records): file:line + normalized_title** - applied when one or both records lack a `content_hash`, preserving existing behavior unchanged.
    90	
    91	### Synthesis Behavior
    92	
    93	When the same `content_hash` appears across iterations from different dimensions, synthesis collapses them to ONE entry with `dimensions: [<all dimensions that emitted it>]` rather than emitting multiple records. Records without `content_hash` fall back to `file:line + normalized_title`; no migration is required for existing JSONL state.
    94	
    95	### Emission Requirement
    96	
    97	Every finding emitted into the JSONL delta (`findingDetails[]`) MUST include a `content_hash` field computed per the two-tier match. `reduce-state.cjs` reads this field for synthesis dedup.
    98	
    99	---
   100	
   101	## 7. RELATED RESOURCES
   102	
   103	- `scripts/reduce-state.cjs` is the reducer implementation.
   104	- `references/state/state_format.md` defines state records.
   105	- `references/state/state_outputs.md` defines operator-visible outputs.
   106	- `references/convergence/convergence_signals.md` defines STOP fields reducer views display.

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	---
     2	title: Deep Research Reducer And Registry Reference
     3	description: Reducer ownership, findings registry schema, validation, fault tolerance, and reconstruction rules.
     4	---
     5	
     6	# Deep Research Reducer And Registry Reference
     7	
     8	The reducer turns append-only iteration state into synchronized strategy, registry, dashboard, and synthesis metadata. It is the source of truth for derived packet state.
     9	
    10	---
    11	
    12	## 1. OVERVIEW
    13	
    14	### Purpose
    15	
    16	Define reducer ownership, findings registry shape, validation behavior, fault tolerance, file protection, dashboard derivation, and legacy aliases.
    17	
    18	### When to Use
    19	
    20	Load this reference when rebuilding derived state, validating registry/dashboard outputs, or deciding whether a file is agent-owned or reducer-owned.
    21	
    22	### Core Principle
    23	
    24	The reducer owns derived state. Manual edits to reducer-managed outputs are temporary and may be overwritten.
    25	
    26	---
    27	
    28	## 2. REDUCER CONTRACT
    29	
    30	The workflow reducer:
    31	
    32	- reads `deep-research-state.jsonl`, per-iteration deltas, strategy, and iteration markdown;
    33	- validates required iteration artifacts;
    34	- updates machine-owned strategy sections;
    35	- regenerates the findings registry;
    36	- regenerates the dashboard;
    37	- records convergence and blocked-stop summaries.
    38	
    39	It must fail closed before writing derived files when JSONL corruption is detected in strict mode.
    40	
    41	---
    42	
    43	## 3. FINDINGS REGISTRY
    44	
    45	The canonical registry stores open/resolved questions, findings, ruled-out directions, convergence state, and blocked-stop history.
    46	
    47	```json
    48	{
    49	  "openQuestions": [],
    50	  "resolvedQuestions": [],
    51	  "keyFindings": [],
    52	  "ruledOutDirections": [],
    53	  "coverageBySources": {},
    54	  "convergenceScore": 0.42,
    55	  "blockedStopHistory": [],
    56	  "graphConvergenceScore": null,
    57	  "graphDecision": null,
    58	  "graphBlockers": [],
    59	  "lastUpdated": "2026-05-24T00:00:00Z"
    60	}
    61	```
    62	
    63	The currently documented live registry name is `deep-research-findings-registry.json` in command state paths, with `findings-registry.json` still appearing in older docs and packet examples. New docs should prefer the command state path and mention the older name only when explaining compatibility.
    64	
    65	---
    66	
    67	## 4. VALIDATION RULES
    68	
    69	Blocking validation:
    70	
    71	- config, state log, and strategy exist before a resume;
    72	- every dispatched iteration produces a non-empty iteration markdown file;
    73	- every dispatched iteration appends a JSONL delta record with required fields;
    74	- JSONL records parse in strict mode;
    75	- reducer-owned outputs refresh after iteration evaluation;
    76	- convergence STOP candidates record gate evidence.
    77	
    78	Post-dispatch failure codes include:
    79	
    80	| Code | Meaning |
    81	|------|---------|
    82	| `iteration_file_missing` | Expected iteration markdown file was not created |
    83	| `iteration_file_empty` | Iteration markdown exists but is empty |
    84	| `jsonl_not_appended` | JSONL state did not receive the iteration record |
    85	| `jsonl_missing_fields` | Required JSONL fields are absent |
    86	| `jsonl_parse_error` | JSONL line is malformed |
    87	
    88	Three consecutive failures route to stuck recovery or escalation.
    89	
    90	---
    91	
    92	## 5. FAULT TOLERANCE
    93	
    94	Fault-tolerant reads may skip malformed JSONL lines only in lenient recovery mode. Strict mode stops before derived writes.
    95	
    96	Safe recovery order:
    97	
    98	1. Preserve the corrupted state file.
    99	2. Read valid JSONL records.
   100	3. Reconstruct missing iteration summaries from `iterations/iteration-NNN.md`.
   101	4. Append explicit reconstructed records.
   102	5. Regenerate registry and dashboard.
   103	6. Record the recovery in JSONL.
   104	
   105	Example reconstructed record:
   106	
   107	```json
   108	{"type":"iteration","run":3,"status":"reconstructed","focus":"extracted focus","findingsCount":0,"newInfoRatio":0.0}
   109	```
   110	
   111	---
   112	
   113	## 6. FILE PROTECTION MAP
   114	
   115	The config may include:
   116	
   117	```json
   118	{
   119	  "fileProtection": {
   120	    "deep-research-config.json": "immutable",
   121	    "deep-research-state.jsonl": "append-only",
   122	    "deep-research-strategy.md": "mutable",
   123	    "deep-research-dashboard.md": "auto-generated",
   124	    "deep-research-findings-registry.json": "auto-generated",
   125	    "iteration-*.md": "write-once",
   126	    "research/research.md": "mutable"
   127	  }
   128	}
   129	```
   130	
   131	Protection is a workflow contract. It documents ownership and supports validation; it is not a substitute for command-level checks.
   132	
   133	---
   134	
   135	## 7. DASHBOARD DERIVATION
   136	
   137	Dashboard values are derived from raw state:
   138	
   139	| Dashboard Field | Source |
   140	|-----------------|--------|
   141	| iteration count | JSONL iteration records |
   142	| current status | latest lifecycle or synthesis event |
   143	| convergence trend | `convergenceSignals` |
   144	| blocked stops | registry `blockedStopHistory` |
   145	| graph convergence | registry graph fields |
   146	| next focus | strategy |
   147	
   148	`synthesis_complete` events are authoritative for terminal status when present.
   149	
   150	---
   151	
   152	## 8. LEGACY ALIASES
   153	
   154	The workflow may dual-read older names during migration windows, but new writes use canonical `deep-research-*` artifacts. Migration reads should append a migration event that records `legacyPath`, `canonicalPath`, and timestamp.

  1160	        log: "Iteration {N} complete. Dimensions: {dimensions}. newFindingsRatio: {newFindingsRatio}. Findings: P0={severity_counts.P0} P1={severity_counts.P1} P2={severity_counts.P2}. Stuck count: {stuck_count}."
  1161	
  1162	      step_loop_decision:
  1163	        action: "Return to step_read_state for next iteration"
  1164	        goto: step_read_state
  1165	
  1166	  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  1167	  # PHASE 3: SYNTHESIS
  1168	  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  1169	  phase_synthesis:
  1170	    purpose: Compile all iteration findings into review/review-report.md
  1171	    steps:
  1172	
  1173	      step_fanout_merge:
  1174	        description: "Merge N lineage registries with strongest-restriction; bind p0/p1/p2 counts for step_derive_verdict; skip when single-executor"
  1175	        skip_when: "config.fanout is absent"
  1176	        command: |
  1177	          node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-merge.cjs \
  1178	            --loop-type review \
  1179	            --artifact-dir {artifact_dir}
  1180	        working_directory: "{repo_root}"
  1181	        wait_for_completion: true
  1182	        bind_from_output:
  1183	          p0_count: "active_p0"
  1184	          p1_count: "active_p1"
  1185	          p2_count: "active_p2"
  1186	
  1187	      step_hydrate_summary_metrics:
  1188	        action: "Hydrate summary metrics before synthesis"
  1189	        read:
  1190	          - "{state_paths.config}"
  1191	          - "{state_paths.state_log}"
  1192	          - "{state_paths.findings_registry}"
  1193	          - "{state_paths.strategy}"
  1194	        extract:
  1195	          - iteration_count: "Count lines where type === 'iteration'"
  1196	          - p0_count: "Total P0 findings across all iterations (deduped)"
  1197	          - p1_count: "Total P1 findings across all iterations (deduped)"
  1198	          - p2_count: "Total P2 findings across all iterations (deduped)"
  1199	          - dimensions_covered: "List of reviewed dimensions"
  1200	          - dimension_coverage: "covered / total"
  1201	          - last_3_summaries: "Summarize the latest 3 iteration records"
  1202	          - reason: "Use the latest stop reason if present, else the frozen default 'converged'. (Must be a member of the STOP_REASONS enum: converged, maxIterationsReached, blockedStop, stuckRecovery, error, manualStop, userPaused.)"
  1203	
  1204	      # H-1 verdict derivation: parse findings JSONL, map highest severity to verdict
  1205	      # - PASS: no P0 or P1 findings (P2-only or clean)
  1206	      # - CONDITIONAL: any P1 findings (no P0)
  1207	      # - FAIL: any P0 findings
  1208	      # Final line of iteration-NNN.md MUST be exactly: "Review verdict: PASS" / "Review verdict: CONDITIONAL" / "Review verdict: FAIL"
  1209	      step_derive_verdict:
  1210	        action: "Derive review verdict from accumulated findings"
  1211	        algorithm: |
  1212	          if p0_count > 0:
  1213	            verdict = "FAIL"
  1214	          elif p1_count > 0:
  1215	            verdict = "CONDITIONAL"
  1216	          else:
  1217	            verdict = "PASS"
  1218	          has_advisories = (verdict == "PASS" and p2_count > 0)
  1219	        outputs:
  1220	          - verdict: "PASS | CONDITIONAL | FAIL"
  1221	          - has_advisories: "Boolean true when verdict is PASS and any active P2 findings exist"
  1222	        contract: |
  1223	          Every iteration-NNN.md must end with exactly: "Review verdict: PASS" (or CONDITIONAL or FAIL).
  1224	          Downstream automation parses this final line via exact string match — do not vary the format.
  1225	
  1226	      step_read_all_iterations:
  1227	        action: "Read all iteration files and strategy"
  1228	        read_pattern: "{state_paths.iteration_dir}/iteration-*.md"
  1229	        read_also: "{state_paths.strategy}"
  1230	
  1231	      step_build_finding_registry:
  1232	        action: "Build deduplicated finding registry from all iterations"
  1233	        algorithm: |
  1234	          1. Extract all findings from iteration JSONL delta files
  1235	          2. Compute content_hash for each finding (emission requirement):
  1236	             content_hash = sha256(file_path + "\u001f" + line_range + "\u001f" + finding_type + "\u001f" + normalized_description_80chars)
  1237	             Normalized description = whitespace-collapsed, lowercased, first 80 chars of the finding description.
  1238	          3. Two-tier dedup:
  1239	             PRIMARY: group by content_hash field
  1240	               - Same content_hash, multiple dimensions → single finding, merged `dimensions: [<all>]` array
  1241	               - Keep highest severity on duplicates
  1242	             FALLBACK (legacy records lacking content_hash): file:line + normalized_title
  1243	               - Same file:line, multiple dimensions → single finding, multiple dimension tags
  1244	               - Keep highest severity on duplicates
  1245	          4. Track finding lifecycle:
  1246	             - status: active | duplicate | upgraded | downgraded | contested | resolved_false_positive
  1247	             - Record first_seen, upgraded_in, previous_severity for severity changes
  1248	             - Record dimensions array reflecting all dimensions that emitted this finding
  1249	          5. Resolve contradictions:
  1250	             - Findings marked contested across iterations → referee pass
  1251	             - Determine final severity and status
  1252	          6. Count only 'active' findings for totals
  1253	        outputs:
  1254	          - finding_registry: "Deduplicated list of all findings with metadata"
  1255	          - active_p0: "Count of active P0 findings"
  1256	          - active_p1: "Count of active P1 findings"
  1257	          - active_p2: "Count of active P2 findings"
  1258	
  1259	      step_adversarial_selfcheck:
  1260	        action: "Final adversarial self-check on all P0 and P1 findings"
  1261	        algorithm: |
  1262	          For each active P0/P1 finding:
  1263	            1. Hunter: Re-verify finding against source code (re-read file:line)
  1264	            2. Skeptic: Challenge severity — is this really P0/P1? Could it be P2?
  1265	            3. Referee: Final call — confirm, downgrade, or mark false_positive

  1268	      step_emit_resource_map:
  1269	        action: "Emit {state_paths.resource_map_output} from converged review deltas unless config.resource_map.emit is false"
  1270	        command: "node .opencode/skills/deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs {spec_folder} --emit-resource-map"

  1272	          - "{state_paths.config}"
  1273	          - "{state_paths.delta_dir}/iter-*.jsonl"
  1274	        write:
  1275	          - "{state_paths.resource_map_output}"
  1276	        guarantee: "Reducer skips cleanly when config.resource_map.emit == false and writes a deterministic resource-map when enabled."
  1277	
  1278	      step_resource_map_coverage_gate:
  1279	        action: "If resource_map_present, compute the Resource Map Coverage Gate synthesis inputs; otherwise preserve the skip note"
  1280	        note: "Cross-check target_files from {spec_folder}/applied/T-*.md against {spec_folder}/resource-map.md. Report touched entries, untouched entries split into expected-by-scope vs gap, and implementation paths absent from the map. Findings raised here use category resource-map-coverage with severity calibrated to the gate outcome."
  1281	
  1282	      step_compile_review_report:
  1283	        action: "Compile review/review-report.md with 9 core sections, a conditional Resource Map Coverage Gate section, and an embedded Planning Packet"
  1284	        instructions: |
  1285	          Create review/review-report.md with the following sections:
  1286	
  1287	          1. **Executive Summary**
  1288	             - Overall verdict: PASS | CONDITIONAL | FAIL
  1289	             - hasAdvisories: true when PASS carries active P2 items
  1290	             - P0/P1/P2 counts (active only)
  1291	             - Review scope summary
  1292	
  1293	          2. **Planning Trigger**
  1294	             - State whether `/speckit:plan` is required
  1295	             - Include a fenced `json` block labeled `Planning Packet`
  1296	             - The packet MUST include: `triggered`, `verdict`, `hasAdvisories`, `activeFindings`, `remediationWorkstreams`, `specSeed`, `planSeed`, `findingClasses`, `affectedSurfacesSeed`, `fixCompletenessRequired`
  1297	             - Build `activeFindings`, `findingClasses`, and `affectedSurfacesSeed` from reducer-owned state plus iteration JSONL `findingDetails`; if a field is missing, mark it `UNKNOWN` instead of inferring it from prose
  1298	
  1299	          3. **Active Finding Registry**
  1300	             - Unified registry for active P0/P1/P2 findings
  1301	             - For each: ID, severity, title, dimension, file:line, evidence, impact, fix recommendation, disposition, findingClass, scopeProof, affectedSurfaceHints
  1302	
  1303	          4. **Remediation Workstreams**
  1304	             - Ordered action groups: P0 first, then P1, with P2 advisories separated
  1305	
  1306	          5. **Spec Seed**
  1307	             - Concrete seed bullets for follow-on spec updates
  1308	
  1309	          6. **Plan Seed**
  1310	             - Concrete starter tasks for `/speckit:plan`
  1311	
  1312	          7. **Traceability Status**
  1313	             - Summarize required core protocols first, then applicable overlay protocols
  1314	             - Include status, evidence, and any unresolved drift per protocol
  1315	
  1316	          If `resource_map_present` is true, insert **## Resource Map Coverage Gate** after `Traceability Status` with exactly three bullets:
  1317	             - `Entries touched`: count of resource-map paths that appear in applied reports
  1318	             - `Entries not touched`: paths in resource-map not in implementation, labeled `expected-by-scope` or `gap`
  1319	             - `Implementation paths absent from resource-map`: `target_files` in applied reports not on the map
  1320	
  1321	          8. **Deferred Items**
  1322	             - Advisory-only or backlog-worthy follow-ups that do not block the current verdict
  1323	
  1324	          9. **Search Ledger**
  1325	             - Always include this section before the appendix.
  1326	             - Render reducer-owned `searchCoverage`, `candidateCoverage`, `searchDebt`, `ruledOutCandidates`, and `cleanSearchProof`.
  1327	             - If all arrays are empty or absent, include: `*No search-depth state captured (legacy v1 record)*`.
  1328	             - When `searchDebt` is non-empty, state `hasSearchDebt: true` and carry the dashboard verdict as CONDITIONAL unless active P0 findings make it FAIL.
  1329	
  1330	          10. **Audit Appendix**
  1331	             - Convergence summary, coverage summary, ruled-out claims, sources reviewed
  1332	             - Cross-reference appendix MUST be split into `Core Protocols` and `Overlay Protocols`
  1333	
  1334	          Preserve machine-owned markers so the reducer-owned registry, dashboard, and strategy anchors can be refreshed on re-run without mutating prior synthesis. (Note: `completed-continue` lineage branch is deferred — see `deep-review/references/protocol/loop_protocol.md §Lifecycle Branches`.)
  1335	        output: "{state_paths.review_output}"
  1336	
  1337	      step_convergence_report:
  1338	        action: "Append convergence report to JSONL"
  1339	        append_to_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"synthesis_complete","mode":"review","totalIterations":{iteration_count},"activeP0":{active_p0},"activeP1":{active_p1},"activeP2":{active_p2},"dimensionCoverage":{dimension_coverage},"verdict":"{verdict}","releaseReadinessState":"{release_readiness_state}","stopReason":"{reason}","timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}"}'
  1340	        note: "Emit as numeric ratio (e.g., 0.75), not quoted string. Compute: dimensions_covered / total_dimensions."
  1341	
  1342	      step_update_config_status:
  1343	        action: "Mark config as complete"
  1344	        edit: "{state_paths.config}"
  1345	        set_field: { status: "complete" }
  1346	
  1347	      step_stage_artifact_dir:
  1348	        action: "Stage all skill-owned artifacts under artifact_dir for the operator's next git commit"
  1349	        command: "git add {state_paths.artifact_dir}"
  1350	        note: "Skill-owned contract fix (028): without this stage step, operators who do `git add review-report.md` lose the iteration trail (iterations/, deltas/, state.jsonl, strategy, findings-registry, etc.). Staging the whole artifact_dir keeps the audit trail intact. Operators retain commit authority and can `git restore --staged {path}` to unstage anything before commit. Failure is non-fatal — log warning and continue."
  1351	        on_fail: "Log warning. Do not block synthesis. The artifact_dir may be outside a git repo (e.g., temp test fixture) which is a valid case."
  1352	
  1353	  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  1354	  # PHASE 4: SAVE
  1355	  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  1356	  phase_save:
  1357	    purpose: Preserve review context to memory system
  1358	    steps:
  1359	
  1360	      step_generate_context:

     1	---
     2	title: "Phase 005: Consumer-specific merges + synthesis hooks"
     3	description: "New deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-merge.cjs (research: dedup + cross-model attribution; review: severity rollup + strongest-restriction any-lineage-P0-blocks) + step_fanout_merge atop both phase_synthesis; fanout-attribution.md."
     4	trigger_phrases:
     5	  - "123 phase 005 merge"
     6	  - "fanout-merge.cjs synthesis"
     7	importance_tier: "important"
     8	contextType: "implementation"
     9	_memory:
    10	  continuity:
    11	    packet_pointer: "skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/005-consumer-merges-synthesis"
    12	    last_updated_at: "2026-05-30T00:00:00Z"
    13	    last_updated_by: "claude-opus"
    14	    recent_action: "Phase 5 complete — fanout-merge.cjs + step_fanout_merge in all 4 YAMLs + 10 tests; 196/197 (loop-lock flake only)"
    15	    next_safe_action: "Phase 6: command surface + docs + final parity gate (006-command-surface-docs-parity)"
    16	    blockers: []
    17	    completion_pct: 100
    18	    open_questions: []
    19	    answered_questions: []
    20	---
    21	<!-- SPECKIT_TEMPLATE_SOURCE: spec-core | v2.2 -->
    22	<!-- SPECKIT_LEVEL: 2 -->
    23	
    24	# Phase 005 — Consumer-specific merges + synthesis hooks
    25	
    26	## Purpose
    27	Consolidate the N isolated lineage packets into ONE canonical output per consumer, reusing the existing synthesis compilers.
    28	
    29	## Scope
    30	- New `deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-merge.cjs` (`--loop-type research|review`), reads every `{artifact_dir}/lineages/{label}/` registry + iterations + state log.
    31	- New `step_fanout_merge` at the TOP of each `phase_synthesis` (4 YAMLs), gated on `config.fanout`:
    32	  - research: dedup (reuse reducer content-hash) + cross-model attribution → consolidated `deep-research-findings-registry.json` → existing `step_compile_research` emits canonical 17-section `research.md`.
    33	  - review: severity rollup, **strongest-restriction** (any-lineage active P0 ⇒ merged FAIL) → consolidated `deep-review-findings-registry.json` → existing synthesis emits 9-section `review-report.md` + verdict.
    34	  - Both write `{artifact_dir}/fanout-attribution.md` (per-lineage convergence, iters, salvage events, model attribution).
    35	
    36	## Success
    37	- Unit: research dedup/attribution; review rollup + strongest-restriction (A clean, B P0 ⇒ merged FAIL).
    38	- Integration: 2-lineage fixture → merged registry → existing synthesis → canonical report unchanged in shape.
    39	
    40	## Out of scope
    41	Command flags + docs + final parity (006).

   954	  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   955	  # PHASE 3: SYNTHESIS
   956	  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   957	  phase_synthesis:
   958	    purpose: Compile all iteration findings into final research/research.md
   959	    steps:
   960	
   961	      step_fanout_merge:
   962	        description: "Merge N lineage registries into one consolidated registry; skip when single-executor"
   963	        skip_when: "config.fanout is absent"
   964	        command: |
   965	          node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-merge.cjs \
   966	            --loop-type research \
   967	            --artifact-dir {artifact_dir}
   968	        working_directory: "{repo_root}"
   969	        wait_for_completion: true
   970	
   971	      step_hydrate_summary_metrics:
   972	        action: "Hydrate summary metrics before synthesis"
   973	        read:
   974	          - "{state_paths.config}"
   975	          - "{state_paths.state_log}"
   976	          - "{state_paths.registry}"
   977	          - "{state_paths.strategy}"
   978	        extract:
   979	          - iteration_count: "Count lines where type === 'iteration'"
   980	          - current_iteration: "iteration_count + 1"
   981	          - current_segment: "Latest segment_start event segment, else 1"
   982	          - last_focus: "Extract focus from latest iteration record, else 'none yet'"
   983	          - ratio_prev: "Extract newInfoRatio from second-latest iteration record, else 'N/A'"
   984	          - ratio_latest: "Extract newInfoRatio from latest iteration record, else 'N/A'"
   985	          - remaining_questions: "Count unchecked items in 'Key Questions'"
   986	          - remaining_questions_list: "List unchecked key questions from strategy.md"
   987	          - answered_count: "Count answered questions from strategy.md"
   988	          - total_questions: "Count total questions tracked in strategy.md"
   989	          - last_3_summaries: "Summarize the latest 3 iteration records as 'run N: focus (ratio)'"
   990	          - reason: "Use the latest synthesis/recovery stop reason if present, else the frozen default 'converged'. (Must be a member of the STOP_REASONS enum: converged, maxIterationsReached, blockedStop, stuckRecovery, error, manualStop, userPaused.)"
   991	
   992	      step_read_all_iterations:
   993	        action: "Read all iteration files and strategy"
   994	        read_pattern: "{state_paths.iteration_dir}/iteration-*.md"
   995	        read_also: "{state_paths.strategy}"
   996	
   997	      step_emit_resource_map:
   998	        action: "Emit {state_paths.resource_map_output} from converged research deltas unless config.resource_map.emit is false"
   999	        command: "node .opencode/skills/deep-research/scripts/reduce-state.cjs {spec_folder} --emit-resource-map"
  1000	        read:
  1001	          - "{state_paths.config}"
  1002	          - "{state_paths.delta_dir}/iter-*.jsonl"
  1003	        write:
  1004	          - "{state_paths.resource_map_output}"

   900	  const { artifactDir: researchDir } = resolveArtifactRoot(resolvedSpecFolder, 'research');
   901	  const configPath = path.join(researchDir, 'deep-research-config.json');
   902	  const stateLogPath = path.join(researchDir, 'deep-research-state.jsonl');
   903	  const strategyPath = path.join(researchDir, 'deep-research-strategy.md');
   904	  const registryPath = path.join(researchDir, 'findings-registry.json');
   905	  const dashboardPath = path.join(researchDir, 'deep-research-dashboard.md');
   906	  const resourceMapPath = path.join(researchDir, 'resource-map.md');
   907	  const iterationDir = path.join(researchDir, 'iterations');
   908	  const deltaDir = path.join(researchDir, 'deltas');
   909	
   910	  const config = readJson(configPath);
   911	  const { records: parsedRecords, corruptionWarnings } = parseJsonlDetailed(readUtf8(stateLogPath));
   912	  const records = parsedRecords.filter((record) => record.type === 'iteration');
   913	  const events = parsedRecords.filter((record) => record.type === 'event');
   914	  const strategyContent = readUtf8(strategyPath);
   915	  const strategyQuestions = parseStrategyQuestions(strategyContent);
   916	  const iterationFiles = fs.existsSync(iterationDir)
   917	    ? fs.readdirSync(iterationDir)
   918	        .filter((fileName) => /^iteration-\d+\.md$/.test(fileName))
   919	        .sort((a, b) => {
   920	          // DR-006: numeric sort on trailing iter number; default .sort() puts iteration-10.md between iteration-1.md and iteration-2.md.
   921	          const numA = parseInt(a.match(/iteration-(\d+)\.md$/)?.[1] ?? '0', 10);
   922	          const numB = parseInt(b.match(/iteration-(\d+)\.md$/)?.[1] ?? '0', 10);
   923	          return numA - numB;
   924	        })
   925	        .map((fileName) => parseIterationFile(path.join(iterationDir, fileName)))
   926	    : [];
   927	
   928	  const terminalStop = buildTerminalStopState(parsedRecords);
   929	  const lineage = buildLineageState(config, events);
   930	  config.lineage = {
   931	    ...(config.lineage && typeof config.lineage === 'object' ? config.lineage : {}),
   932	    sessionId: lineage.sessionId,
   933	    parentSessionId: lineage.parentSessionId,
   934	    lineageMode: lineage.lineageMode,
   935	    generation: lineage.generation,
   936	    continuedFromRun: lineage.continuedFromRun,
   937	  };
   938	  const status = deriveDashboardStatus(config, records, events, terminalStop);
   939	  const registry = buildRegistry(strategyQuestions, iterationFiles, records, events, {
   940	    lineage,
   941	    terminalStop,
   942	    status,
   943	  });
   944	  // Expose corruptionWarnings as a top-level registry field for parity with
   945	  // deep-review (REQ-015 research-side follow-up).
   946	  registry.corruptionWarnings = corruptionWarnings;
   947	  registry.status = status;
   948	  const strategy = updateStrategyContent(strategyContent, registry, iterationFiles, records);
   949	  const dashboard = renderDashboard(config, registry, records, iterationFiles);
   950	  let resourceMap = null;
   951	  let resourceMapSkipped = true;
   952	  let resourceMapSkipReason = null;
   953	
   954	  if (corruptionWarnings.length > 0 && !lenient) {
   955	    throw createCorruptionError(stateLogPath, corruptionWarnings);
   956	  }
   957	
   958	  if (emitResourceMapOutput) {
   959	    if (getResourceMapEmitSetting(config) === false) {
   960	      resourceMapSkipReason = 'config.resource_map.emit=false';
   961	    } else {
   962	      const deltaPayloads = loadDeltaPayloads(deltaDir);
   963	      if (!deltaPayloads.length) {
   964	        resourceMapSkipReason = 'no delta files found';
   965	      } else {
   966	        resourceMap = emitResourceMap({
   967	          shape: 'research',
   968	          deltas: deltaPayloads,
   969	          packet: {
   970	            title: config.topic || path.basename(resolvedSpecFolder),
   971	            specFolder: resolvedSpecFolder,
   972	          },
   973	          scope: `research convergence output for ${path.basename(resolvedSpecFolder)}`,
   974	          createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),
   975	        });
   976	        resourceMapSkipped = false;
   977	      }
   978	    }
   979	  }
   980	
   981	  if (write) {
   982	    writeUtf8(registryPath, `${JSON.stringify(registry, null, 2)}\n`);
   983	    writeUtf8(strategyPath, strategy.endsWith('\n') ? strategy : `${strategy}\n`);
   984	    writeUtf8(dashboardPath, dashboard);
   985	    if (emitResourceMapOutput && resourceMap) {
   986	      writeUtf8(resourceMapPath, resourceMap);
   987	    }
   988	  }
   989	
   990	  return {
   991	    configPath,
   992	    stateLogPath,
   993	    strategyPath,
   994	    registryPath,
   995	    dashboardPath,
   996	    resourceMapPath,
   997	    registry,
   998	    strategy,
   999	    dashboard,
  1000	    resourceMap,
  1001	    resourceMapSkipped,
  1002	    resourceMapSkipReason,
  1003	    corruptionWarnings,
  1004	    hasCorruption: corruptionWarnings.length > 0,
  1005	  };
  1006	}
  1007	
  1008	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1009	// 5. CLI ENTRY POINT
  1010	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1011	
  1012	if (require.main === module) {
  1013	  const args = process.argv.slice(2);
  1014	  const lenient = args.includes('--lenient');
  1015	  const emitResourceMapOutput = args.includes('--emit-resource-map');
  1016	  const positional = args.filter((arg) => !arg.startsWith('--'));
  1017	  const specFolder = positional[0];
  1018	
  1019	  if (!specFolder) {
  1020	    process.stderr.write(
  1021	      'Usage: node .opencode/skills/deep-research/scripts/reduce-state.cjs <spec-folder> [--lenient] [--emit-resource-map]\n',
  1022	    );
  1023	    process.exit(1);
  1024	  }
  1025	
  1026	  try {
  1027	    const result = reduceResearchState(specFolder, { write: true, lenient, emitResourceMap: emitResourceMapOutput });
  1028	    process.stdout.write(
  1029	      `${JSON.stringify(
  1030	        {
  1031	          registryPath: result.registryPath,
  1032	          dashboardPath: result.dashboardPath,
  1033	          strategyPath: result.strategyPath,
  1034	          iterationsCompleted: result.registry.metrics.iterationsCompleted,
  1035	          corruptionCount: result.corruptionWarnings.length,
  1036	          resourceMapPath: emitResourceMapOutput ? result.resourceMapPath : null,
  1037	          resourceMapSkipped: emitResourceMapOutput ? result.resourceMapSkipped : null,
  1038	          resourceMapSkipReason: emitResourceMapOutput ? result.resourceMapSkipReason : null,
  1039	        },
  1040	        null,
  1041	        2,
  1042	      )}\n`,
  1043	    );
  1044	    // Fail-closed exit semantics matching deep-review (REQ-015 parity).
  1045	    if (result.hasCorruption && !lenient) {
  1046	      process.exit(2);
  1047	    }
  1048	  } catch (error) {
  1049	    if (error && error.code === 'STATE_CORRUPTION') {
  1050	      process.stderr.write(`${error.message}\n`);
  1051	      process.exit(2);
  1052	    }
  1053	    process.stderr.write(`[deep-research] reducer failed: ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)}\n`);
  1054	    process.exit(3);
  1055	  }
  1056	}
  1057	
  1058	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1059	// 6. EXPORTS
  1060	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1061	
  1062	module.exports = {
  1063	  buildGraphConvergenceRollup,
  1064	  buildLineageState,
  1065	  buildTerminalStopState,
  1066	  computeGraphConvergenceScore,
  1067	  deriveDashboardStatus,
  1068	  parseIterationFile,
  1069	  parseJsonl,
  1070	  parseJsonlDetailed,
  1071	  reduceResearchState,

  1779	
  1780	if (require.main === module) {
  1781	  const args = process.argv.slice(2);
  1782	  const lenient = args.includes('--lenient');
  1783	  const createMissingAnchors = args.includes('--create-missing-anchors');
  1784	  const emitResourceMapOutput = args.includes('--emit-resource-map');
  1785	  const positional = args.filter((arg) => !arg.startsWith('--'));
  1786	  const specFolder = positional[0];
  1787	
  1788	  if (!specFolder) {
  1789	    process.stderr.write(
  1790	      'Usage: node .opencode/skills/deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs <spec-folder> [--lenient] [--create-missing-anchors] [--emit-resource-map]\n',
  1791	    );
  1792	    process.exit(1);
  1793	  }
  1794	
  1795	  try {
  1796	    const result = reduceReviewState(specFolder, {
  1797	      write: true,
  1798	      lenient,
  1799	      createMissingAnchors,
  1800	      emitResourceMap: emitResourceMapOutput,
  1801	    });

  1803	      `${JSON.stringify(
  1804	        {

  1807	          strategyPath: result.strategyPath,
  1808	          openFindingsCount: result.registry.openFindingsCount,

  1816	          resourceMapSkipReason: emitResourceMapOutput ? result.resourceMapSkipReason : null,
  1817	        },
  1818	        null,
  1819	        2,
  1820	      )}\n`,
  1821	    );
  1822	  } catch (error) {

  1824	      process.stderr.write(`${error.message}\n`);
  1825	      process.exit(2);
  1826	    }
  1827	    process.stderr.write(`[deep-review] reducer failed: ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)}\n`);
  1828	    process.exit(3);
  1829	  }
  1830	}

    98	  packet_dir: "{artifact_dir}"

   760	          wait_for_completion: true

   790	            optional_variant_flag: |

   792	              Otherwise expand to empty string.
   793	          notes:
   794	            - "opencode run takes a positional message; prompt is fed via command substitution"
   795	            - "--variant maps reasoningEffort to opencode's provider-specific reasoning lever (e.g., high, medium, minimal)"
   796	            - "--dangerously-skip-permissions enables hands-off automation; sandboxMode='read-only' is not currently honored (no opencode equivalent)"
   797	            - "cli-opencode self-invocation guard: this branch must only run from a non-OpenCode dispatch surface (Claude/Codex/Gemini/raw shell). The cli-opencode skill SKILL.md §SELF-INVOCATION PROHIBITED contract is the authoritative gate"
   798	            - "</dev/null required: opencode v1.14.39 reads stdin at startup before session creation. Without explicit closed stdin, automation that redirects stdout/stderr hangs at 0% CPU after `+60s snapshot prune cleanup`. See cli-opencode SKILL.md §4 ALWAYS rule + memory feedback_opencode_run_requires_dev_null_stdin.md."
   799	        if_cli_devin:
   800	          command: |
   801	            AGENT_CONFIG_SRC="{repo_root}/.opencode/skills/cli-devin/assets/agent-config-deep-research-iter.json"
   802	            AGENT_CONFIG_TMP="{state_paths.prompt_dir}/agent-config-iter-{current_iteration}.json"
   803	            DEVIN_STDERR_TMP="{state_paths.prompt_dir}/devin-stderr-iter-{current_iteration}.log"
   804	            DEVIN_STDOUT_TMP="{state_paths.prompt_dir}/devin-stdout-iter-{current_iteration}.log"
   805	            sed -e "s|<repo-root>|{repo_root}|g" -e "s|<packet-root>|{spec_folder}|g" "$AGENT_CONFIG_SRC" > "$AGENT_CONFIG_TMP"
   806	            gtimeout 900 devin --print \
   807	              --prompt-file "{state_paths.prompt_dir}/iteration-{current_iteration}.md" \
   808	              --model "{config.executor.model}" \
   809	              --permission-mode "{resolved_permission_mode}" \
   810	              --agent-config "$AGENT_CONFIG_TMP" \
   811	              </dev/null \
   812	              > "$DEVIN_STDOUT_TMP" 2> "$DEVIN_STDERR_TMP"
   813	            DEVIN_EXIT=$?
   814	            cat "$DEVIN_STDOUT_TMP"
   815	            cat "$DEVIN_STDERR_TMP" >&2
   816	            if [ $DEVIN_EXIT -eq 124 ]; then
   817	              echo "ITER_OUTCOME=timeout" >&2
   818	            elif grep -q "A tool was rejected by the user" "$DEVIN_STDERR_TMP" 2>/dev/null; then
   819	              echo "ITER_OUTCOME=tool_rejected" >&2
   820	            elif grep -qE "(I cannot write|read-only mode|permission errors when attempting to write)" "$DEVIN_STDOUT_TMP" 2>/dev/null; then
   821	              echo "ITER_OUTCOME=degraded_stdout_fallback" >&2
   822	            else
   823	              echo "ITER_OUTCOME=clean" >&2
   824	            fi
   825	            exit $DEVIN_EXIT

   735	
   736	      step_reduce_state:
   737	        action: "Run the reducer to synchronize registry, dashboard, and strategy"
   738	        command: "node .opencode/skills/deep-research/scripts/reduce-state.cjs {spec_folder}"
   739	        inputs:
   740	          latestJSONLDelta: "Latest JSONL records written since the previous iteration"

   742	          priorReducedState: "{state_paths.registry}"
   743	        outputs:
   744	          - "{state_paths.registry}"
   745	          - "{state_paths.dashboard}"
   746	          - "{state_paths.strategy}"
   747	        failure_modes:
   748	          malformed_delta: "skip + warn"
   749	          missing_iteration_file: "no-op + error"
   750	          schema_mismatch: "reject + conflict event"
   751	        guarantee: "Idempotent for identical inputs"
   752	
   753	      step_graph_upsert:
   754	        action: "Persist the latest iteration graph delta after the reducer refresh and after the iteration file exists"
   755	        read:
   756	          - "{state_paths.state_log}"
   757	        extract:
   758	          - latest_graph_events: "Read graphEvents from the latest iteration record, else []"
   759	          - graph_upsert_nodes_json: "Map latest_graph_events where type == 'node' to node payloads with label -> name and preserve remaining node fields"
   760	          - graph_upsert_edges_json: "Map latest_graph_events where type == 'edge' to edge payloads with source -> sourceId, target -> targetId, relation -> uppercase(relation), and preserve remaining edge fields"
   761	          - graph_upsert_event_count: "Count mapped node and edge events"
   762	        if_graph_events_present:
   763	          bash: "node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/upsert.cjs --spec-folder \"{spec_folder}\" --loop-type \"research\" --session-id \"{config.lineage.sessionId}\" --nodes '{graph_upsert_nodes_json}' --edges '{graph_upsert_edges_json}'"
   764	          note: "Skip silently when graphEvents is missing or empty."
   765	        if_graph_events_missing:
   766	          proceed: true
   767	
   768	      step_evaluate_results:
   769	        action: "Verify iteration outputs and extract metrics"
   770	        verify:
   771	          - file_exists: "{state_paths.iteration_pattern}"
   772	          - jsonl_appended: "New iteration record in state.jsonl"
   773	          - strategy_updated: "Reducer refreshed strategy machine-owned sections"
   774	          - registry_updated: "Reducer refreshed deep-research-findings-registry.json"
   775	        extract:

   777	          - findings_count: "From latest JSONL iteration record"
   778	          - status: "From latest JSONL iteration record"
   779	          - latest_focus: "From latest JSONL iteration record"
   780	        on_missing_outputs:

   782

codex
## Focus
fanout-merge.cjs consumer merges

## Findings
P0, .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-merge.cjs:284, The research branch reads `deep-research-findings-registry.json`, but the deep-research reducer writes `findings-registry.json` at `.opencode/skills/deep-research/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:904`. Fan-out research can therefore merge the initialized/stale registry and drop the reducer-produced findings. Fix: align the reducer, YAML state path, and fanout merge on one registry filename, or have the merge read the reducer-owned filename with a compatibility fallback.

P1, .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-merge.cjs:97, Research dedup keys on `finding.id`, but reducer-produced IDs include lineage-local iteration/index data, so the same finding discovered in different lineages will not dedup and `_lineages` attribution is split. Fix: dedup by `contentHash` when present, otherwise normalized `text`, and merge sources/lineages.

P1, .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-merge.cjs:174, Review dedup keys on lineage-local `findingId`/`title`, ignoring the reducer’s content-hash contract and fallback key (`contentHash`, else file:line + title). Different findings can collide on common local IDs like `F001`, while the same finding with different local IDs duplicates. Fix: reuse the reducer dedup key and merge dimensions, merged IDs, evidence, and lineage attribution.

P1, .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-merge.cjs:65, Malformed JSON is swallowed as `null`, and lineages with null registries are skipped at line 305. A review lineage containing an active P0 but a malformed registry can be omitted, allowing a merged PASS/CONDITIONAL despite the strongest-restriction contract. Fix: distinguish missing from parse-failed registries and fail closed on parse errors.

## Verdict
The merge path is not safe to ship for fan-out consumers until the registry source and dedup keys are corrected.
Review verdict: FAIL

===FINDINGS_JSON===
[{"id":"f-p123-i3-01","severity":"P0","file":".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-merge.cjs","line":284,"issue":"The research branch reads deep-research-findings-registry.json, but the deep-research reducer writes findings-registry.json, so fan-out research can merge stale or empty registries and drop reducer-produced findings.","fix":"Align the reducer, YAML state path, and fanout merge on one registry filename, or have fanout-merge read the reducer-owned filename with a compatibility fallback."},{"id":"f-p123-i3-02","severity":"P1","file":".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-merge.cjs","line":97,"issue":"Research dedup keys on finding.id, whose reducer-produced value is lineage-local, so identical findings from different lineages do not dedup and attribution is split.","fix":"Dedup by contentHash when present, otherwise normalized text, and merge sources plus lineage attribution."},{"id":"f-p123-i3-03","severity":"P1","file":".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-merge.cjs","line":174,"issue":"Review dedup keys on lineage-local findingId/title instead of the reducer content-hash contract, causing unrelated local IDs to collide and same findings with different local IDs to duplicate.","fix":"Use contentHash primary and file:line plus normalized title fallback, preserving highest severity while merging dimensions, merged IDs, evidence, and lineages."},{"id":"f-p123-i3-04","severity":"P1","file":".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-merge.cjs","line":65,"issue":"Malformed registry JSON is swallowed as null and later skipped, so a lineage with an active P0 can be omitted from the merged review verdict.","fix":"Return a distinct parse-failure state and fail closed on malformed lineage registries instead of counting them as skipped."}]
===END_FINDINGS===

## Focus
fanout-merge.cjs consumer merges

## Findings
P0, .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-merge.cjs:284, The research branch reads `deep-research-findings-registry.json`, but the deep-research reducer writes `findings-registry.json` at `.opencode/skills/deep-research/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:904`. Fan-out research can therefore merge the initialized/stale registry and drop the reducer-produced findings. Fix: align the reducer, YAML state path, and fanout merge on one registry filename, or have the merge read the reducer-owned filename with a compatibility fallback.

P1, .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-merge.cjs:97, Research dedup keys on `finding.id`, but reducer-produced IDs include lineage-local iteration/index data, so the same finding discovered in different lineages will not dedup and `_lineages` attribution is split. Fix: dedup by `contentHash` when present, otherwise normalized `text`, and merge sources/lineages.

P1, .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-merge.cjs:174, Review dedup keys on lineage-local `findingId`/`title`, ignoring the reducer’s content-hash contract and fallback key (`contentHash`, else file:line + title). Different findings can collide on common local IDs like `F001`, while the same finding with different local IDs duplicates. Fix: reuse the reducer dedup key and merge dimensions, merged IDs, evidence, and lineage attribution.

P1, .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-merge.cjs:65, Malformed JSON is swallowed as `null`, and lineages with null registries are skipped at line 305. A review lineage containing an active P0 but a malformed registry can be omitted, allowing a merged PASS/CONDITIONAL despite the strongest-restriction contract. Fix: distinguish missing from parse-failed registries and fail closed on parse errors.

## Verdict
The merge path is not safe to ship for fan-out consumers until the registry source and dedup keys are corrected.
Review verdict: FAIL
